# chezmoi-dotfiles

This machine's `$HOME` is managed by a **chezmoi** (configs) + **ansible** (system
deps) dotfiles repo. This skill is the agent-facing map: how to drive it, and what
custom tooling exists that you wouldn't otherwise know about. It is rendered per-host
— the "What's enabled on THIS machine" section reflects what the user actually
selected at init.

## Orientation (read this first)

- **Repo root**: `chezmoi source-path` (no args) prints the source dir. Work there
  to edit: `cd "$(chezmoi source-path)"`.
- **Source path of one file**: `chezmoi source-path ~/.config/foo/bar` → the
  managed source file for that target.
- **`docs/**` is NOT deployed to `$HOME`** (it's chezmoi-ignored). Read detail via
  `"$(chezmoi source-path)/docs/..."`. A *subset* is deployed to `~/.config/docs/`
  (e.g. keybindings, cli-tools) for runtime helpers.
- **Read the contract before editing**: `"$(chezmoi source-path)/CLAUDE.md"` holds
  Hard invariants and cross-file maintenance rules. Honor them.
- This host: profile `macos_intel`, OS `darwin`/`amd64`, primary shell `zsh`.

## chezmoi essentials

```bash
chezmoi diff                 # preview changes (also: just chezmoi-diff)
chezmoi apply                # deploy + run scripts (also: cas, or just chezmoi-apply)
chezmoi status               # modified/added/removed targets
chezmoi edit ~/.zshrc        # edit the SOURCE of a target
chezmoi add  ~/.config/x     # start managing an existing file
chezmoi re-add               # pull live edits back into source
chezmoi managed | grep foo   # is X managed?
chezmoi cat ~/.config/x      # render a target without applying
chezmoi data | jq            # the template data (ground truth for the toggles below)
```

- **`cas`** = `chezmoi apply` + reload hint; **`cau`** = update from remote then apply
  (defined in `dot_config/shell/99_chezmoi_reload.sh`).
- **`dotcfg`** = reconfigure the init prompts on an already-set-up machine
  (`dotcfg` for the TUI, `dotcfg --set installLlmTools=true --yes` for one toggle,
  `dotcfg --dry-run` to preview). Shell twin: `czcfg`.
- **Never `chezmoi add` a `modify_`/`create_` target** — it overwrites the script.
- Full command cheat-sheet: `docs/this_repo/cheatsheet.md`. Install-vs-upgrade is
  split: `chezmoi apply`/ansible is **install-only**; upgrades go through
  `just upgrade-*`.

## What's enabled on THIS machine

(Rendered from this host's selections. For live truth: `chezmoi data | jq`.)
- **Coding agents** — Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, Codex, Gemini, Copilot (configs + CLIs). See `docs/tools/agent-overlays.md`, `docs/tools/agent-skills.md`.
- **Python uv tools** — mlflow, marimo, jupyter, etc. (powers the `mlf` CLI). See `docs/this_repo/uv-bootstrap.md`.
- **Extra runtimes** — rust, bun, ruby via mise (+ cargo/gem tools). Node is always present.
- **LLM tools** — Ollama / LiteLLM / local models.
- **JS CLI tools** — standalone npm CLIs (e.g. readability-cli).
- **.NET tools** — .NET SDK (mise) + dotnet global tools.
- **IaC** — Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu.
- **Media** — ffmpeg, ImageMagick, exiftool, libvips.
- **Media control** — nowplaying-cli + switchaudio-osx (macOS) / playerctl (Linux); unlocks full `sysplay`/`sysnow` (built-in `sysvol`/`sysmute` work regardless).
- **Networking** — nmap, mtr, httpie, gping, trippy.
- **Tunnels** — ngrok, cloudflared.
- **Vim mode** — shell modal editing + tmux vim nav (NOT Neovim/editors — those are always vim).

## Custom in-house CLIs (always deployed to `~/.dotfiles/bin`, on PATH)

Each works from any cwd. Run with `--help`, or read `docs/tools/<name>.md`. Full list: `ls "$(chezmoi source-path)/dot_dotfiles/bin/"`.

- **`fleet`** — cross-host ops over your machines: `fleet chezmoi apply`, `fleet hosts`, `fleet tmux`, `fleet info`, `fleet pueue`, `fleet exec`. See `docs/this_repo/fleet-apply.md`, `docs/tools/fleet-exec.md`, `docs/tools/fleet-hosts.md`.
- **`mlf`** — MLflow umbrella CLI + TUI dashboard (needs Python uv tools). Picker twin: `tv mlflow`.
- **`pqsum`** — pueue queue summary (text / AI / JSON). See `docs/tools/pueue.md`.
- **`x`** — clipboard/file copy-paste-open with cross-platform fallbacks.
- **`mi-router`** / **`reyee`** / **`sms`** — read-only router CLIs (Xiaomi MiWiFi / Ruijie Reyee / Huawei HiLink SMS). See `docs/tools/{mi-router,reyee,sms}.md`.
- **`ping-monitor`** — record ping over time, flag latency spikes. See `docs/tools/ping-monitor.md`.
- **`yth`** — search your YouTube watch history (title/channel/description + captions; Takeout import + optional cookie sync). Picker twin: `tv yth`. See `docs/tools/yth.md`.
- **`dotcfg`** — reconfigure dotfiles prompts (above).
- **`sesh-preview`** — preview helper for the sesh picker.

## Television (`tv`) — fuzzy pickers with custom channels

There are ~40 **custom** channels (system state, fleet, services, infra, agents…)
that you won't discover otherwise. Browse them:

```bash
tv                      # default picker
tv list-channels        # list everything available
tv <channel>            # open one, e.g. tv fleet-hosts | tv mlflow | tv pueue
ls "$(chezmoi source-path)/dot_config/television/cable/"   # the channel definitions
```

Handy ones: `fleet-hosts`, `mlflow`, `pueue`, `ports`, `kill-process`, `services`,
`sessions`, `keybindings`, `aliases`, `git-ops`, `mac-apps`/`linux-apps`. Full
reference: `docs/tools/tv.md`.

## Keybindings & keymaps

- **`bindings`** (shell function) or **`tv keybindings`** — interactive reference.
- **tmux**: `prefix + Space` (or `prefix + e`) opens the action menu;
  `prefix + T` = sesh picker, `prefix + U` = tools. Deep ref: `docs/tools/tmux/keybindings.md`.
- **shell ZLE widgets**: `Alt+S` sesh sessions, `Alt+T` tools, `Ctrl+R` history (atuin).
- Master reference: `docs/shells/keybindings.md` (also deployed to `~/.config/docs/shells/keybindings.md`).

## Aliases & functions

`alias` to list shell aliases, or `tv aliases`. Catalog: `docs/shells/aliases.md`.
Helpers worth knowing: `chezmoi-cd` (jump to source), `run-for` (time-box a command),
`bindings` (above). App control: `applaunch`/`appquit`/`apprestart`/… (cross-platform).
System audio (cross-platform, built-in): `sysvol`/`sysmute` (+ `sysplay`/`sysnow` with `installMediaControl`); remote mute via `fleet exec --login -- sysmute on`.

## just recipes (run from the source dir)

```bash
cd "$(chezmoi source-path)" && just --list
```

Categories: `chezmoi-*`, `ansible-*`, `upgrade-*` (brew/mise/uv/npm/cargo/…),
`fleet`/`fleet-*`, `gen-prompts` (regenerate prompt scaffolding), `check`/`lint`,
`docker-*`. See `docs/this_repo/cheatsheet.md`.

## Editing rules (don't skip)

Before changing anything under the repo, read `CLAUDE.md` Hard invariants and the
"Cross-file maintenance rules" table — many surfaces have mandatory mirror updates
in the same commit (docs, completions, tool-managers index, this very skill, …).

===== go:embed =====
===== Justfile =====
# translate — dev tasks (run `just` to list)

# show available recipes
default:
    @just --list

# build the binary into ./translate
build:
    go build -o translate .

# run: `just run "hola mundo" --to en`  ·  `just run` for the TUI
run *ARGS:
    go run . {{ARGS}}

# launch the interactive TUI
tui:
    go run .

# dictionary lookup: `just define ephemeral`
define WORD:
    go run . define {{WORD}}

# guided config wizard
init:
    go run . init

# gofmt, vet, and build
check: fmt vet build

# format the source tree
fmt:
    gofmt -w cmd internal main.go

# static analysis
vet:
    go vet ./...

# tidy go.mod / go.sum
tidy:
    go mod tidy

# run tests
test:
    go test ./...

# install into the chezmoi bin dir (first on PATH)
install: build
    install -m 0755 translate ~/.dotfiles/bin/translate

# remove build artifacts
clean:
    rm -f translate
===== xdgpath =====
// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
//
// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
package xdgpath

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
const app = "translate"

// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
func base(env, def string) string {
	if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
		return v
	}
	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		home = "."
	}
	return filepath.Join(home, def)
}

// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }

// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }

// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }

// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }

// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }

// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }

// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
func EnsureDirs() error {
	for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
		if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
			return err
		}
	}
	return nil
}

```text
===== dictupdate.go (how dict is obtained) =====
package cmd

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"translate/internal/config"
	"translate/internal/engine"
)

func newDictCmd() *cobra.Command {
	c := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "dict",
		Short: "Manage the local bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT)",
	}
	c.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "update [cedict|ecdict|all]",
		Short: "Download/build the local dictionaries (~67 MB one-time)",
		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
		RunE:  runDictUpdate,
	})
	return c
}

func runDictUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	cfg, _, err := config.Load()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	what := "all"
	if len(args) == 1 {
		what = args[0]
	}
	dir := engine.DictDir(cfg.Dict.Dir)
	ctx := cmd.Context()
	prog := func(s string) { fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  "+s) }

	if what == "cedict" || what == "all" {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "CC-CEDICT (Chinese→English):\n")
		if err := engine.DownloadCedict(ctx, cfg.Dict.CedictURL, engine.CedictPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("cedict: %w", err)
		}
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.CedictPath(dir))
	}
	if what == "ecdict" || what == "all" {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ECDICT (English→Chinese, this takes a minute):\n")
		if err := engine.BuildEcdictDB(ctx, cfg.Dict.EcdictURL, engine.EcdictDBPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("ecdict: %w", err)
		}
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.EcdictDBPath(dir))
	}
	if what != "cedict" && what != "ecdict" && what != "all" {
		return fmt.Errorf("unknown target %q (use cedict|ecdict|all)", what)
	}
	fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "done. Dictionary mode (^e) is now bilingual zh↔en.")
	return nil
}

```text
===== .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl (how externals are declared) =====
# https://www.chezmoi.io/reference/special-files-and-directories/chezmoiexternal-format/
# https://www.chezmoi.io/user-guide/include-files-from-elsewhere/
#
# Upstream sources that used to be cloned/downloaded by Ansible are declared
# here so chezmoi can refresh them automatically on `chezmoi apply`.
#
# refreshPeriod = "168h" -> re-pull once a week. Force an immediate refresh
# with `chezmoi apply --refresh-externals`.
#
# All git-repo entries use --depth 1 (shallow) and --ff-only on pull so a
# dirty working tree in the clone won't be clobbered silently.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Oh-My-Zsh core + custom plugins
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Nested externals are safe: chezmoi does `git pull` on refresh (not re-clone),
# so plugin subdirs under .oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/ are preserved.

[".oh-my-zsh"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.git"
    refreshPeriod = "168h"
    [".oh-my-zsh".clone]
        args = ["--depth", "1"]
    [".oh-my-zsh".pull]
        args = ["--ff-only"]

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions.git"
    refreshPeriod = "168h"
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions".clone]
        args = ["--depth", "1"]
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions".pull]
        args = ["--ff-only"]

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.git"
    refreshPeriod = "168h"
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting".clone]
        args = ["--depth", "1"]
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting".pull]
        args = ["--ff-only"]

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-completions"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-completions.git"
    refreshPeriod = "168h"
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-completions".clone]
        args = ["--depth", "1"]
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-completions".pull]
        args = ["--ff-only"]

{{ if .enableVimMode -}}
[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-vi-mode"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/jeffreytse/zsh-vi-mode.git"
    refreshPeriod = "168h"
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-vi-mode".clone]
        args = ["--depth", "1"]
    [".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-vi-mode".pull]
        args = ["--ff-only"]
{{- end }}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Oh-My-Bash core (sister of oh-my-zsh; all platforms).
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cloned regardless of primaryShell so `bash` works ad-hoc on zsh-primary
# hosts. Source order in dot_bashrc.tmpl is presence-gated, so a missing
# clone (mid-bootstrap, network failure) just falls back to plain bash +
# starship. OMB's autosuggestions / syntax-highlighting / history-substring-
# search plugins are deliberately NOT enabled in dot_config/bash/01_omb_plugins
# because ble.sh provides better native versions and double-init causes
# flicker + duplicate-completion bugs.
#
# We do NOT use OMB's installer (`tools/install.sh`) — it destructively `mv`s
# ~/.bashrc to .bashrc.omb-backup-<date>. chezmoi already manages dot_bashrc,
# so we just clone OMB's source tree and source oh-my-bash.sh from our bashrc.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 17:26:28Z)**_

看完了。這個 CLI 跟你 dotfiles 裡現有的工具有個關鍵差異值得先講清楚，它決定了「好方法」是哪一種。

## 現況與關鍵約束

- **它是編譯出來的 binary**（22MB，Mach-O x86_64），而你 `~/.dotfiles/bin/` 裡目前**全部都是文字腳本**（uv script / bash）。那個目錄是直接 commit 進 chezmoi git repo 的 —— 把 22MB 的編譯產物塞進去是反模式：撐大 repo、而且綁死平台（你的 fleet 是多主機多架構，這台是 `macos_intel`/amd64，別台跑不動）。
- **字典資料不在 binary 裡**。`translate dict update` 會在執行期下載 ~67MB 到 `~/.local/share/translate/dict`。所以 binary 本身是自足的、可跨編譯的（`modernc.org/sqlite` 是純 Go,沒有 cgo,`GOOS/GOARCH` 交叉編譯無痛)。
- 你的 dotfiles 對「編譯型工具」的慣例是:**不 commit binary**,而是透過 ansible/brew/mise/cargo 安裝、記在 `docs/this_repo/tool-managers.md`、用 `just upgrade-*` 升級。而且 `~/go/bin`(GOPATH/bin)**已經在 PATH 上**(`02_legacy_tools.sh`)。

所以「常駐 binary」的正解是:**用 chezmoi 管 config,binary 交給一個安裝機制在 `chezmoi apply` 時產生**,而不是把 binary 當 dotfile。Justfile 裡那個 `just install`(複製到 `~/.dotfiles/bin/`)只適合這台當快速 hack —— 它是未受管檔案,不會傳到 fleet、新機器 clean apply 也不會有。

## 建議的兩條路(取決於你要不要 push 到 GitHub)

**A. 推到 GitHub → `go install`(最乾淨,如果你願意 publish)**
```
go install github.com/<you>/translate@latest   # 落在 ~/go/bin,已在 PATH
```
升級就是重跑 `@latest`。代價:你現在的 tool-managers 裡**還沒有 go 這個安裝機制**(只有 cargo/npm/mise/uv),所以要照 CLAUDE.md 在 `tool-managers.md` 新增一個 go 分類的 row + decision tree。適合當它的長期歸宿。

**B. 保持 private/本地 → `.chezmoiexternal` clone + `run_onchange` build(零發佈基建)**
- 在 `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl` 加一個 git-repo external,把 repo clone 到 `~/.local/src/translate`。

.specstory/history/2026-07-09_11-04-39Z-cli-tui-typo-framework.md:382:9	      "Verified. Final recommendations below.\n\n## 1. Google unofficial endpoint — CONFIRMED, use as free-path default\n`GET https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single?client=gtx&sl=<auto|src>&tl=<tgt>&dt=t&q=<urlenc>`\n\nLive response for `sl=auto&tl=es&q=hello world` (with `dt=t&dt=bd&dt=at`):\n```\n[[[\"Hola Mundo\",\"hello world\",null,null,10]],null,\"en\",null,null,\n [[\"hello world\",null,[[\"Hola Mundo\",null,true,false,[10]],[\"hola mundial\",null,true,false,[8]]],[[0,11]],\"hello world\",0,0]],\n 0.76348495,[],[[\"en\"],null,[0.76348495],[\"en\"]]]\n```\nParsing (nested-array positions are stable across the projects that use this):\n- **Full translation** = concatenate `data[0][i][0]` for all i (segment 0 = translated chunk, segment 1 = original chunk). Never assume one segment — long text splits into many.\n- **Detected source lang** = `data[2]` (`\"en\"`). Confidence at `data[8][2][0]` (~0.76).\n- **`dt` params are additive and each adds a top-level slot**: `dt=t` (translation, index 0), `dt=bd` = **b**ilingual **d**ictionary block at `data[1]` (per-POS: `[posLabel, [terms...], [[term, [reverseTranslations], score, freq]...], ...]`) — only populated for single words/lemmas, `null` for phrases like \"hello world\"; `dt=at` = **a**lternate translations at `data[5]`. You can pass multiple `dt=` in one call.\n- Recommendation: request `client=gtx&dt=t&dt=bd&dt=at` always; parse index 0 (required), 2 (detect), and opportunistically 1/5 to enrich the Marvin-lite `Alternatives[]`.\n\nCaveats: undocumented/unofficial, can break without notice; community reports **HTTP 429** after bursts and occasional 403/captcha. Treat as best-effort: single request per debounce (the 400ms debounce + cancel-in-flight already throttles well), add exponential backoff+jitter on 429, and fall through to the next engine on any non-200. Set a browser-ish `User-Agent`; `client=gtx` avoids the `tk` token requirement.\n\nAlternative free option — **MyMemory** (CONFIRMED, good secondary): `GET https://api.mymemory.translated.net/get?q=<text>&langpair=en|es`\n```json\n{\"responseData\":{\"translatedText\":\"Hola Mundo\",\"match\":1},\"responseStatus\":200,\n \"quotaFinished\":false,\"matches\":[{\"translation\":\"...\",\"quality\":\"...\",\"match\":0.9,...}]}\n```\nSimple flat shape (`responseData.translatedText`, `responseStatus`, `matches[]` for alternatives). Limits: **5,000 chars/day anonymous**, 50,000/day if you add `&de=email@x`; **500 bytes max per `q`**. No auto-detect of source (needs explicit langpair). Recommendation: keep Google unofficial as primary free path (has detect + no daily cap), wire MyMemory as an easy fallback since its shape is trivial. Skip public LibreTranslate instances for v1 — the public ones are flaky/require keys now.\n\n## 2. Bing/Microsoft — DEFER\nNo documented keyless path. The community `https://www.bing.com/ttranslatev3` route requires scraping transient `IG`/`IID`/`token`/`key` from the translator web page first, then POSTing — fragile, ToS-risky, breaks often. The only supported Microsoft path is Azure Translator (`api.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com`, requires Azure subscription key). Recommendation: **do not include Bing in v1**; the LLM chain + Google + MyMemory cover the free need. Leave an engine interface seam so an Azure-key Bing engine can drop in later.\n\n## 3. Dictionary — use dictionaryapi.dev for v1, local wordlist+Levenshtein for fuzzy\nFree Dictionary API (CONFIRMED): `GET https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/en/<word>` → array of entries:\n```\nword, phonetic, phonetics[]{text,audio}, meanings[]{ partOfSpeech,\n  definitions[]{definition, example?, synonyms[], antonyms[]}, synonyms[], antonyms[] },\nlicense{name,url}, sourceUrls[]\n```\nOn miss it returns **HTTP 404** (confirmed for `helllo`) with a `{title,message,resolution}` body — **no suggestions/nearest-word field**. So the API cannot power fuzzy itself.\n\nFuzzy strategy recommendation: on 404, resolve nearest headword **locally** — ship a bundled English wordlist (e.g. SCOWL/`words` ~50–100k, or the dictionaryapi word set) and rank candidates by Levenshtein/Damerau distance (Go: `github.com/agnivade/levenshtein` or `agext/levenshtein`), optionally pre-filter by a trigram/prefix index for speed, then re-query the API with the top suggestion (mirrors the CLI's cobra \"did you mean\" UX). This keeps it offline-capable and deterministic.\n\nWiktionary REST (`https://en.wiktionary.org/api/rest_v1/page/definition/<word>`) — object keyed by language code (`\"en\"`, `\"fr\"`, …), each value an array of `{partOfSpeech, language, definitions[]{definition, parsedExamples[], examples[]}}`. It's **multi-language** (good later) but definitions contain HTML markup needing stripping, and it's heavier to parse. Recommendation: **v1 = dictionaryapi.dev** (clean JSON, English, synonyms/antonyms/phonetics/audio out of the box); keep Wiktionary REST as a documented option for multi-language dictionary in a later version.\n\n## 4. Offline language detection in Go — recommend whatlanggo for the prototype\n- `github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo` — light, pure-Go, fast, no large model download, trivial API (`whatlang.Detect(text)` → lang + script + confidence). Best fit for a prototype and for the \"auto\" source when Google is offline.\n- `github.com/pemistahl/lingua-go` — most accurate on short/single-word/mixed text, but heavy (large embedded n-gram models, bigger binary, slower init) — matters for a \"single static binary\" goal.\n\nRecommendation: **start with whatlanggo** (`github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo`) for binary size + speed; note lingua-go as an upgrade path behind the same internal `Detector` interface if short-text accuracy proves insufficient. In practice detection is mostly a fallback since Google returns `data[2]` and the LLM returns `DetectedSource` in the structured result — so keeping detection light is the right call.\n\nNote: I did not write a plan file — per instructions these findings are returned directly as my output.\n\nSources: [Free Dictionary API](https://dictionaryapi.dev/), [Wiktionary REST](https://en.wiktionary.org/api/rest_v1/), [MyMemory limits](https://mymemory.translated.net/doc/usagelimits.php), [lingua-go](https://github.com/pemistahl/lingua-go), [whatlanggo](https://github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo), [Azure Translator v3](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/translator/text-translation/reference/v3/translate)."

Currently 14 tools auto-generated: chezmoi/mise/uv/just/starship/gh/docker/rg/fd/bat/delta/zellij/pueue/opencode. NOT auto-generated (handled elsewhere): sesh/tv/worktrunk (shell-startup mtime check), bw/marimo/thefuck/try-cli (cached eval), mi-router (its own startup script + tyro `#compdef` rewrite), fleet/mlf/pqsum/x (hand-written). |
27	| Keybinding (`Ctrl+`/`Alt+`) in any tool config | Cross-check namespace before claiming — tmux root-table `bind -n C-*` shadows inner apps; prefer `Alt+` for new actions | [docs/shells/keybindings.md](docs/shells/keybindings.md), [docs/tools/claude-code-keybindings.md](docs/tools/claude-code-keybindings.md), [docs/this_repo/vim-mode.md](docs/this_repo/vim-mode.md) |
28	| AI agent autodetect order or `AICAP_*_MODEL` defaults | Edit **only** [`dot_config/shell/04_ai_agents.sh`](dot_config/shell/04_ai_agents.sh) — the SSOT. Both shells source it; the **four** Python consumers (`dot_config/tmux/executable_tmux-session-summary.py`, `scripts/aiblock.py`, `dot_dotfiles/bin/executable_pqsum`, `scripts/fleet/exec.py`) regex-parse the same file for cron / tmux-popup contexts where env isn't inherited. Each agent's `-m`/`--model` flag is conditional on its env var being non-empty so a retired model ID falls through to the CLI's own default. When adding a NEW agent, update: SSOT (one new `AICAP_<AGENT>_MODEL` line), `04_ai_capture.sh:_aiagent_invoke` case, **all four** Python `AGENT_CONFIG` dicts. **Four consumers = pain threshold reached** — the extraction TODO (`scripts/aisum/__init__.py`) is now blocking ergonomics; next AI-tooling change should land that refactor before adding more callsites. | — |
29	| chezmoi-apply reload hint: `.chezmoiscripts/global/run_after_99_signal_reload.sh.tmpl` (touches sentinel) ↔ `dot_config/shell/99_chezmoi_reload.sh` (reads sentinel + zsh/bash hook registration + `cas`/`cau` wrappers) | Row in [docs/shells/aliases.md](docs/shells/aliases.md) "Dotfiles management" section for `cas`/`cau` | The sentinel path `~/.cache/chezmoi/last-apply` is hard-coded in **two** places — the run-script that touches it and the shared backend that reads it. Keep both in sync when relocating (e.g. switching to `$XDG_STATE_HOME`). Hook registration is in the shared backend (NOT `dot_config/{zsh,bash}/99_*`) because bash's `$BASH_CONFIG_DIR` loads before `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/shell` (oh-my-bash `plugins=()` prereq, see `dot_bashrc.tmpl:94` vs `:114`), so a per-shell hook file in `dot_config/bash/` would run before the backend function exists. Opt out: `export CHEZMOI_RELOAD_HINT=0`. |
30	| `app-*` helpers — `dot_config/shell/54_macos_apps.sh.tmpl` (macOS) ↔ `dot_config/shell/56_linux_apps.sh.tmpl` (Linux) ↔ `dot_config/television/cable/mac-apps.toml.tmpl` ↔ `dot_config/television/cable/linux-apps.toml.tmpl` | Both shell helpers MUST expose the SAME public verb names (`appquit` / `applaunch` / `appactivate` / `apprestart` / `apprunning` / `applist` / `appresponsive`) so users get cross-platform muscle memory. Backends diverge by necessity (Apple Events vs `gtk-launch` + `pkill -TERM` + optional GNOME `window-calls` ext). Adding a new verb to one side: decide whether it can also be implemented on the other; if not, document the gap in `backlog/linux-desktop-app-control.md` "Open follow-ups". Both `tv` channel files MUST share Alt-key bindings where the verb exists on both sides — **Alt+H is mac-only by design** (Linux can't hide windows without compositor IPC; the Alt+H slot stays free on `tv linux-apps`). Per-app overrides for Linux AppImage/Snap/Flatpak runtime paths live in `~/.config/shell/linux-apps.conf` (not auto-stubbed, matches `.shellrc.secrets` rule) — never auto-generate this file from the helper. | [docs/playbooks/linux-gui-apps.md](docs/playbooks/linux-gui-apps.md) "Controlling installed apps from the shell" section; [pitfalls/linux-app-control-appimage-runtime-path.md](pitfalls/linux-app-control-appimage-runtime-path.md), [pitfalls/linux-app-control-gapplication-zero-coverage.md](pitfalls/linux-app-control-gapplication-zero-coverage.md). Empirical baseline: `gapplication list-apps` returns only ~13 GNOME-core apps on Ubuntu 24.04 Wayland — third-party Electron/AppImage/Snap/Flatpak coverage is **zero**, so don't reach for `gapplication action … quit` as the primary mechanism. |
31	| Docker `registry-mirrors` list in `dot_config/docker/modify_daemon.json.tmpl` (canonical) | Full list + per-mirror notes in [docs/tools/containers.md](docs/tools/containers.md) + `.zh-TW` (Strategy A JSON block + bullets), and the coverage-matrix Docker row + § Security and trust model in [docs/tools/mirrors.md](docs/tools/mirrors.md) + `.zh-TW` | When dropping/adding a mirror, state the **security** reason, not just speed: a pull-through mirror resolves `tag→digest` and Docker Content Trust is off by default, so a dead/third-party mirror domain risks lapsing → re-registration into a malicious pull-through cache. Curated high-reputation set only; `azk8s`/`dockerproxy` removed 2026-07 for this. |
32	
33	**Repo-root files that stay OUT of MkDocs nav** (`README.md`, `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` / `GEMINI.md`, `TODO.md`, `backlog/`, `pitfalls/`, `NOTES.md`): referenced via absolute GitHub URLs from [`docs/for-maintainers.md`](docs/for-maintainers.md), never copied via `pymdownx.snippets`.
34	
35	**Three-tier file placement** for new shell helpers:
36	
37	| Tier | Dir | When |
38	|---|---|---|
39	| Shared | `dot_config/shell/` | POSIX subset, both shells source. `$ZSH_VERSION`/`$BASH_VERSION` source-time dispatch is OK; ZLE widgets / `compdef` / `bindkey` / `setopt` / `read -q` / `${m:t}` / glob qualifiers are NOT (bash would error on source). |
40	| Zsh-only | `dot_config/zsh/` | ZLE widgets, `compdef`, `bindkey`, `setopt`, zsh-vi-mode hooks. |
41	| Bash-only | `dot_config/bash/` | `bind -x` / `ble-bind`, OMB plugin arrays, bash-specific `shopt`s. |
42	
43	When porting a zsh-only helper to bash via ble.sh, extract the shell-agnostic backend into `dot_config/shell/` and keep each shell's widget binding in its own dir.
44	
45	### Workmux status icon integration spans 6 files
46	
47	Touching the `🤖`/`💬`/`✅` per-tmux-window status mechanism requires keeping these 6 surfaces in sync (full story: [docs/tools/workmux.md](docs/tools/workmux.md); pitfall: [pitfalls/workmux-status-leak.md](pitfalls/workmux-status-leak.md)):
48	
49	1. `dot_ansible/roles/devtools/tasks/main.yml` — binary install (brew tap on macOS; GitHub release `.tar.gz` on Linux)
50	2. `dot_config/workmux/config.yaml` — `status_format: false` is load-bearing
51	3. `dot_config/tmux/theme.catppuccin.conf` — `#{?@workmux_status, #{@workmux_status},}` appended to `@catppuccin_window_text` and `@catppuccin_window_current_text`
52	4. `dot_claude/modify_settings.json` — `Stop`/`SubagentStop`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`Notification` hooks calling `workmux set-window-status` (hook-aware merger preserves CodeIsland entries)
53	5. `dot_config/opencode/plugins/workmux-status.ts` + `dot_config/opencode/modify_package.json`
54	6. `dot_config/shell/60_tmux_status.sh` — generic POSIX `tmux_status_set/get/clear/clear_all/list/run` for non-agent producers
55	
56	**Hard rules**: never run `workmux setup` on a managed machine (would write parallel hook entries); never flip `status_format: true` (fights catppuccin's window-text override); never drop the `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && … || true` guard in Claude hooks (spams errors on fresh boxes); never drop explicit `set-window-status done` from `Stop`/`SubagentStop` (upstream only ever sets `working`, never clears — that's the by-design leak documented in the pitfall). `wt` (worktrunk, `37_worktrunk.zsh`) and `wm` (workmux, `38_workmux.zsh`) stay distinct — different config formats, killer features, helper files.
57	
58	### Long-term backlog + past pitfalls → use the `project-knowledge-harness` skill
59	
60	This repo uses the `project-knowledge-harness` agent skill (restored on every `chezmoi apply` via `.chezmoiscripts/global/run_onchange_after_40_install_global_skills.sh.tmpl`; see [docs/tools/agent-skills.md](docs/tools/agent-skills.md)). **Load the skill** for the template, disambiguation table, and upgrade path.
61	
62	**Fallback rules** so this section still binds when the skill cannot be loaded:
63	
64	- Three repo-root surfaces, all chezmoi-ignored, all NOT auto-redacted: [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) (priority/effort tags `P1`/`P2`/`P3`/`P?` × `S`/`M`/`L`/`XL`), [`backlog/`](backlog/README.md) (research/design notes for `P?` / `[L]` / `[XL]` / multi-option), [`pitfalls/`](pitfalls/README.md) (debugged traps — **title by symptom, not root cause**, verbatim error messages, never paraphrase).
65	- Capture triggers — future work: "maybe later", "nice to have", "工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來". Pitfalls: >~15 min debugging, not googleable, non-obvious fix, silent failure.
66	- Do **not** spawn `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `LESSONS.md` / `TROUBLESHOOTING.md`. Three surfaces, always.
67	- A pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant below when it (a) recurs across machines, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) has a non-obvious workaround. Link back to `pitfalls/<slug>.md`.
68	
69	## Chezmoi templating conventions
70	
71	**Hard rule**: before adding `{{ if eq .profile … }}`, ask if the predicate is auto-detectable. If yes, use `.chezmoi.os` / `.chezmoi.arch` / `.chezmoi.hostname`. `.profile` is for user-role choices only. Values are limited to `macos`, `ubuntu_desktop`, `ubuntu_server` — do **not** introduce new values for OS/arch facts (historical `macos_intel` profile was removed for exactly this reason).
72	
73	**Hard rule — prefer XDG paths.** When a tool supports both a `$HOME`-root dotfile and an XDG path (`~/.config/<tool>/…`), manage the XDG copy (`dot_config/<tool>/…`) to keep `$HOME` tidy; only use a root dotfile when the tool can't read XDG. For tools that error when *both* paths exist (e.g. AeroSpace), also remove the legacy root file via a gated `.chezmoiremove` entry. Full rule + lookup-order caveats: [docs/this_repo/config-conventions.md → A1](docs/this_repo/config-conventions.md).
74	
75	| Predicate | Use |
76	|---|---|
77	| Any macOS | `eq .chezmoi.os "darwin"` |
78	| Any Linux | `eq .chezmoi.os "linux"` |
79	| Apple Silicon | `eq .chezmoi.arch "arm64"` (in darwin scope) or `and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (eq .chezmoi.arch "arm64")` |
80	| Intel Mac | `and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (eq .chezmoi.arch "amd64")` |
81	| Desktop vs headless | `.profile` — `ubuntu_desktop` / `ubuntu_server` |
82	
83	Decision table, before/after examples, `macos_intel` migration: [docs/tools/chezmoi-templating.md](docs/tools/chezmoi-templating.md).
84	
85	## Hard repo invariants
86	
87	### Validate app configs with the app, not just syntax
88	
89	Template rendering / YAML / TOML / JSON syntax is not enough. If the app exposes a config check, parser, dry-run, or debug command, run it against the rendered config before declaring done (e.g. `codex debug …` for `~/.codex/config.toml`; `tmux source-file` / `display-menu` smoke for tmux; `mkdocs build --strict` for docs; for Ansible run the narrowest practical play/tag/check-mode or container smoke — `--syntax-check` alone is a first pass). If a validator cannot be run, say so explicitly in the final report and name the missing command/host/credential.
90	
91	### `primaryShell` choice gates `chsh` only — both shells always deploy
92	
93	`primaryShell` (`zsh` | `bash`, default `zsh`) **only** governs which login shell `chsh` switches to. Both `~/.zshrc`, `~/.bashrc`, `~/.config/{shell,zsh,bash}/` deploy everywhere — users routinely drop into the other shell ad-hoc.
94	
95	**Hard rules**:
96	
97	- Do **not** add `{{ if eq .primaryShell … }}` gates around `dot_zshrc.tmpl`, `dot_bashrc.tmpl`, or `dot_config/{shell,zsh,bash}/**`.
98	- Do **not** gate the zsh / bash ansible roles' **package install** task on `primary_shell` — only `chsh` is gated. macOS bash 5.x brew install + `/etc/shells` whitelist are gated on `primary_shell == "bash"` (zsh-primary mac users skip the brew formula).
99	- Do **not** rely on `$SHELL` — it reflects `/etc/passwd`, lags `chsh` until next login. Use `$ZSH_VERSION` / `$BASH_VERSION`.
100	
101	ble.sh + oh-my-bash 12-step init order in `dot_bashrc.tmpl` is load-bearing: `ble.sh --attach=none` MUST source before bash-preexec/starship; `ble-attach` MUST be the last call before secrets/adhoc; OMB's `autosuggestions` / `syntax-highlighting` / `history-substring-search` plugins MUST be excluded (ble.sh provides natives; double-init causes flicker). Full breakdown: [docs/shells/bash.md](docs/shells/bash.md).
102	
103	### Three-tier user-local override layer
104	
105	User overrides live in **six** untracked files: shared POSIX (`~/.shellrc.adhoc`, `~/.shellrc.secrets`) and per-shell (`~/.zshrc.adhoc`, `~/.bashrc.adhoc`, `~/.config/zsh/secrets.zsh`, `~/.config/bash/secrets.sh`). All six in `.chezmoiignore.tmpl`. Default target: shared. Drop to per-shell only when shell-specific syntax is required.
106	
107	**Load order** in both `dot_zshrc.tmpl` and `dot_bashrc.tmpl`:
108	
109	```
110	managed modules → shared secrets → per-shell secrets → shared adhoc → per-shell adhoc
111	```
112	
113	Secrets before adhoc (so adhoc can read secret vars); shared before per-shell (per-shell wins on conflict).
114	
115	**Hard rules**: never add the shared files to chezmoi management; never auto-stub the `*.secrets` files (empty secrets footgun); the shared adhoc stub heredoc lives in BOTH `dot_zshrc.tmpl` AND `dot_bashrc.tmpl` (first shell to run wins; second sees the file and skips) — keep both heredoc bodies in sync. Full matrix: [docs/shells/adhoc-and-secrets.md](docs/shells/adhoc-and-secrets.md).
116	
117	### `enableVimMode` gates shell + tmux vim, NOT Neovim or editors
118	
119	`enableVimMode` (bool, default `true`) gates **shell modal editing + tmux vim navigation only**. Neovim and every editor config (VSCode/Cursor/Antigravity/Codex/OpenCode/Cursor-CLI) are **never** affected. Full catalog of the 7 gated templated files + 2 first-seed files (`marimo.toml`, btop's `btop.conf`): [docs/this_repo/vim-mode.md](docs/this_repo/vim-mode.md).
120	
121	**Hard rules**:
122	
123	- Do **not** gate anything under `dot_config/nvim/` — Neovim is inherently vim by design.
124	- Do **not** gate editor configs (`.chezmoitemplates/editor/`, `dot_codex/modify_config.toml.tmpl`, `dot_config/opencode/modify_*.json.tmpl`, `dot_cursor/modify_cli-config.json`) — the flag's semantic is shells + tmux only. Editors want vim → install the editor's vim extension.
125	- Do **not** broaden the gate to `bindkey -M viins`/`vicmd` calls inside `dot_config/zsh/tools/*.zsh` — harmless no-ops without zsh-vi-mode loaded.
126	- The `minimal` bundle in `scripts/init/dotfiles_init.py` forces `enableVimMode = False` (CI/Docker). Don't change without explicit user request.
127	- New vim-touched file → follow the "Yes, gate it" / "No, leave it alone" flow in `docs/this_repo/vim-mode.md` → "For maintainers", and add it to the catalog table in that same page.
128	
129	### Install vs upgrade is split on purpose
130	
131	`chezmoi apply` + ansible is **install-only** (`state: present`, `creates:`). Explicit upgrade path: [`scripts/upgrade_tools.sh`](scripts/upgrade_tools.sh) via `just upgrade-*`. `scripts/**` is in `.chezmoiignore.tmpl`, so the upgrade script runs from the repo, never deployed.
132	
133	**Hard rules**:
134	
135	- Do **not** rewrite ansible roles to `state: latest`.
136	- Do **not** add `apt upgrade` / system package bumps to default scope.
137	- **Minimum-version exception**: a role MAY auto-upgrade a single tool inside `chezmoi apply` when it legitimately needs a newer version (e.g. `python_uv_tools` needs `uv >= 0.8.5` for `--with-executables-from`). MUST detect install style (brew vs curl/standalone) and dispatch — never blindly `uv self update` (no-op on brew uv) or `brew upgrade uv` (errors if not a brew formula). Dispatch helper is mirrored in `scripts/upgrade_tools.sh::cat_uv()` and `dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/tasks/main.yml`. See [docs/this_repo/uv-bootstrap.md](docs/this_repo/uv-bootstrap.md), [pitfalls/uv-self-update-homebrew-noop.md](pitfalls/uv-self-update-homebrew-noop.md), [pitfalls/ansible-when-regex-replace-backslash-strip.md](pitfalls/ansible-when-regex-replace-backslash-strip.md).
138	- **`with_executables_from:` entries MUST also declare `extra_binaries:`** in `dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml`. Without it the install guard short-circuits on hosts where the primary binary already exists, and entry-point shims are never written (silent missing `jupyter` / `jupyter-notebook` despite `jupyter-lab` present). Audit: `yq '.python_uv_tools[] | select(.with_executables_from) | select(.extra_binaries == null) | .name' …` MUST print nothing. See [pitfalls/uv-tool-install-creates-guard-misses-executables-from.md](pitfalls/uv-tool-install-creates-guard-misses-executables-from.md).
139	- New upgrade category: [docs/this_repo/upgrades.md → Adding a new category](docs/this_repo/upgrades.md). For a tool already managed by brew/uv/npm/cargo/dotnet/gem/mise: do nothing — generic `upgrade` picks it up.
140	
141	### Sudo session is shared across all run-scripts
142	
143	All three `run_*` scripts share one sudo session via `scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh` — user prompted once at `chezmoi apply` start, reused silently downstream.
144	
145	**Hard rules**:
146	
147	- Do **not** re-implement `sudo -k` / `sudo -v` / TTY-read logic. Call the shared helper.
148	- Do **not** run `sudo -k` (invalidates the cache for the whole flow).
149	- Do **not** register a `trap … EXIT` that removes state (next run-script needs it).
150	- Do **not** read the password into a shell variable. Always `sudo -S <file`.
151	- Do **not** replace `_sudo_spawn_watchdog`'s `setsid`/`nohup` fallback with bare `setsid` — macOS lacks `setsid(1)` in base, the backgrounded failure is silent (redirected to `/dev/null`), and every run-script then re-prompts because `_sudo_state_valid`'s `kill -0` probe finds a dead PID. See [`pitfalls/sudo-shared-setsid-macos.md`](pitfalls/sudo-shared-setsid-macos.md).
152	
153	**Adding a new sudo surface in a run-script**:
154	
155	1. `{{ include "scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh" }}` near the top of the template.
156	2. Set the `NEED_SUDO` template flag.
157	3. Call `sudo_session_init "yourlabel"`; branch on return code + `sudo_session_skip_reason`.
158	4. Privileged commands via `sudo_run …` or `-e @$CHEZMOI_ANSIBLE_BECOME_FILE` for ansible.
159	
160	**Non-interactive password injection** (used by `scripts/fleet/apply.py` over SSH): `sudo_session_init` adopts `CHEZMOI_SUDO_PASSWORD_FILE` (a 0600 file) instead of prompting on `/dev/tty`. **Never** read `CHEZMOI_SUDO_PASSWORD_FILE` directly — always go through `sudo_session_init`. Full API + state + cleanup model: [docs/this_repo/sudo-session.md](docs/this_repo/sudo-session.md).
161	
162	### fleet-apply counter-intuitive defaults
163	
164	Full docs: [`docs/this_repo/fleet-apply.md`](docs/this_repo/fleet-apply.md). Load-bearing invariants:
165	
166	- **`drift` ≠ `failed`**. When chezmoi can't prompt to overwrite a hand-edited file (no PTY over SSH), the host shows yellow ⚠ `drift` with paths listed — but it does **not** count toward exit code. Don't auto-"fix" drift unless asked. Resolution: hand-fix the remote, or `--force` to let template win.
167	- **`fleet-apply-file PATH` skips `run_*` scripts** (uses `chezmoi apply --exclude=scripts`). Ansible / Brewfile changes will NOT execute — use full `fleet-apply` for ansible iterations.
168	- **`--branch BRANCH` is no-op on local hosts** (the local source dir IS the user's editor working tree). Local hosts log warn and run against working tree as-is. The flag also forces mode to `apply`.
169	- **Install-only by design** (inherits the previous invariant). `just upgrade-*` must run on each host; fleet-apply does NOT broadcast upgrades.
170	- **Process substitution + sentinel are load-bearing**: `> >(tee -a $log) 2>&1 & _cz_pid=$!; wait $_cz_pid; _rc=$?; echo $_rc > $sentinel`. `--status` / `--tail` / `--watch` + SIGHUP trap depend on `$_cz_pid` pointing to chezmoi, not tee. Don't change to a pipeline.
171	- **Conservative drift classifier**: `_classify_drift()` only downgrades stderr lines matching the exact `chezmoi: <path>: could not open a new TTY: open /dev/tty:` fingerprint. Anything unrecognised stays `failed`. Don't broaden without explicit user request — silent downgrades hide real errors.
172	- **`fleet-status` ≠ `fleet-apply-status`** — easy to confuse:
173	  - `just fleet-status` → **pre-flight readiness probe** (states: `up-to-date`, `behind`, `ahead`, `dirty`, `drift`, `ready-to-update`, `busy`, `init-in-progress`, `toml-mismatch`, `not-init`, `no-source`, `no-chezmoi`, `unreachable`). Run **before** `fleet-apply`. Always exits 0; use `--readiness-json | jq` for scripted gates. `fleet-status-quick` skips remote `git fetch`.
174	  - `just fleet-apply-status` → **process-liveness probe** of `~/.cache/fleet-apply/<host>.{pid,sentinel,log}`. Run **during/after**. Pairs with `fleet-apply-tail` / `fleet-apply-watch` / `fleet-apply-kill`.
175	  - `toml-mismatch` catches a remote's `chezmoi.toml` missing prompt keys that current `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` requires, via static `_PROMPT_KEY_RE` comparison — `chezmoi dump-config` cannot be used (would re-evaluate the very template we're inspecting).
176	  - `fleet-edit` + `fleet-status` are forgiving (auto-seed / exit 0 with hint); `fleet-apply` keeps strict exit-2 on missing inventory. Don't unify.
177	
178	### `modify_` and `create_` prefix semantics
179	
180	- **`modify_`** files are executable scripts: chezmoi pipes current target contents to stdin, expects new contents on stdout. Used for `~/.claude/settings.json` (jq overlay), editor `settings.json` (VSCode/Cursor/Antigravity), agent CLI configs (`~/.cursor/cli-config.json`, `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, `~/.codex/config.toml`), `~/.config/herdr/config.toml` (tomlkit overlay — managed tables enforced, herdr's runtime writeback preserved), and `~/.gitconfig` (preserves `[credential "<URL>"]` blocks injected by `gh auth setup-git` so the gh-managed absolute path to the local `gh` binary — different on Intel mac / Apple Silicon / Linuxbrew — survives `chezmoi apply`). **Never** `chezmoi add` a `modify_` target — it would overwrite the script with the live file.
181	- **`create_`** files seed once; chezmoi never touches contents after. Used for `~/.config/nvim/lazy-lock.json` and editor `keybindings.json`. Refresh baseline by copying directly: `cp <target> "$(chezmoi source-path <target>)"`. `chezmoi add` strips the prefix; `chezmoi re-add` silently skips.
182	
183	Case studies + presence-gating in `.chezmoiignore.tmpl`: [docs/tools/chezmoi-prefixes.md](docs/tools/chezmoi-prefixes.md). Three agent CLI overlays (incl. the Claude hook-aware merger that coexists with [CodeIsland](https://github.com/wxtsky/CodeIsland)'s auto-installed hook entries without ping-ponging): [docs/tools/agent-overlays.md](docs/tools/agent-overlays.md).
184	
185	### Tmux popup menu: ≥ 3.3 required + must fit terminal height
186	
187	The popup menu (bound to `prefix + Space` and `prefix + e`) is **generated** by `~/.config/tmux/menu.sh`, not inline in `keybindings.conf`. Two unrelated tmux quirks force this:
188	
189	1. **Position clamping (tmux ≥ 3.3)**: `display-menu -x R -y P` is silently suppressed on tmux 3.2a (Ubuntu 22.04 apt) when it would place the menu past the terminal edge. tmux 3.3+ clamps. `devtools` role auto-upgrades on Debian/Ubuntu (Linuxbrew first, else [`nelsonenzo/tmux-appimage`](https://github.com/nelsonenzo/tmux-appimage) extracted to `~/.local/share/tmux-appimage/` with a `~/.local/bin/tmux` shim). Run `tmux kill-server` once after — running servers keep the old binary in memory.
190	2. **Menu-too-tall silent failure (all versions)**: `display-menu` does **not** paginate. Taller than terminal → entire popup suppressed, no error, nothing in `tmux show-messages`. `man tmux`: _"If the menu is too large to fit on the terminal, it is not displayed."_ Misdiagnosed for hours as a CSI-u keysym bug after a 50-row flat menu started failing on smaller heights.
191	
192	**Hard rules**:
193	
194	- **Top menu cap ≈ 14 rows**. Generator reads `#{client_height}` and tier-trims; retest at heights 14 / 22 / 60 when changing tiers. Push lower-frequency items into category submenus.
195	- **Submenus = separate scripts**, each `exec tmux display-menu …`. Quoting context resets per script.
196	- **Complex commands** (anything with `{`, `}`, `;`, backticks, nested fzf binds) MUST live in their own script. tmux's parser treats `{}` / `;` as command-block delimiters even inside `'…'` inside `"…"`, silently aborting parse.
197	- **Test by shrinking the terminal vertically**. Full-height verification does not catch the height-fit failure.
198	
199	See `pitfalls/tmux-display-menu-silent-fail.md` (red herrings included) and `pitfalls/yazi-tmux-popup-crash.md`.
200	
201	### Key tmux settings for coding agents
202	
203	Non-obvious settings other tools depend on; do not remove without checking:
204	
205	- `extended-keys on` + `terminal-features 'xterm*:extkeys'` — forwards Shift+Enter, Ctrl+Enter, Ctrl+/, Ctrl+digit to inner apps (Claude Code, Neovim, …). **Never use `always`** — re-encodes EVERY `Ctrl+letter` as CSI-u including the LF (Ctrl+J) embedded in pasted multi-line text → ble.sh inserts `^[[106;5u` literally instead of newline. See `pitfalls/tmux-extended-keys-always-paste.md`.
206	- `escape-time 0` — eliminates ESC delay for Neovim.
207	- `set-clipboard on` + `terminal-features …:clipboard` (for `xterm*`, `ghostty*`, `alacritty*`) — OSC 52 over SSH without terminfo `Ms`. Paired with SSH-conditional `vim.g.clipboard = vim.ui.clipboard.osc52` in `dot_config/nvim/lua/config/options.lua`. See [docs/tools/tmux/README.md → OSC 52 Clipboard](docs/tools/tmux/README.md).
208	- `allow-passthrough on` — OSC passthrough for terminal images.
209	- macOS terminals must send Option as Meta/Esc+ for `M-` keybindings (theme switching, layouts, fine resize). Ghostty/cmux: `macos-option-as-alt = left` in `dot_config/ghostty/config`. See [docs/tools/ghostty.md](docs/tools/ghostty.md).
210	
211	Full tmux config breakdown / theme switching / keybindings / troubleshooting: [docs/tools/tmux/](docs/tools/tmux/).
212	
213	### Zellij `default_mode "locked"`
214	
215	`dot_config/zellij/config.kdl` uses `default_mode "locked"` so all keys pass through to inner apps by default. `Ctrl+G` to unlock Zellij commands. First Zellij launch (no existing config): select the "Unlock-First (non-colliding)" keybinding preset.
216
```
</details></tool-use>

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Compile + install ble.sh into the runtime dir ~/.local/share/blesh after
3	# the chezmoi external clones the source tree to ~/.local/src/blesh (and on
4	# subsequent weekly refreshes whose pull moves HEAD).
5	#
6	# Trigger: change in the cloned repo's HEAD (commit-hash bump). chezmoi's
7	# `include` template function reads the file at template-render time; we
8	# hash the blesh-source HEAD file. NOTE: must be a stable path inside
9	# the repo clone — if the path changes, this script becomes a no-op
10	# until manual `chezmoi apply --refresh-externals`.
11	#
12	# blesh source HEAD: {{ $bleshHead := joinPath .chezmoi.homeDir ".local/src/blesh/.git/HEAD" -}}
13	# {{ if stat $bleshHead -}}{{ include $bleshHead | sha256sum }}{{ else }}absent{{ end }}
14	#
15	# The `make install` target compiles the runtime ble.sh artefact
16	# (~/.local/share/blesh/ble.sh) FROM the source tree at ~/.local/src/blesh.
17	# Source and install dir are kept separate on purpose: `make install`
18	# rewrites lib/*.sh in place, so installing into the git clone would dirty
19	# it and break the next `git pull --ff-only` (see .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl
20	# + pitfalls/chezmoi-update-blesh-local-changes-overwritten.md). dot_bashrc
21	# .tmpl sources the runtime artefact; without this script the source clone
22	# exists but the runtime doesn't, and bashrc's presence-gate skips ble.sh.
23	
24	set -e
25	export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
26	
27	info() { echo -e "\033[0;34m[INFO]\033[0m $1"; }
28	success() { echo -e "\033[0;32m[SUCCESS]\033[0m $1"; }
29	warn() { echo -e "\033[1;33m[WARN]\033[0m $1"; }
30	
31	BLESH_SRC="$HOME/.local/src/blesh"
32	
33	if [[ ! -d "$BLESH_SRC" ]]; then
34	    info "ble.sh source not cloned yet (skip — chezmoi external may be in-flight)"
35	    exit 0
36	fi
37	
38	if ! command -v make >/dev/null 2>&1; then
39	    warn "make not found; cannot compile ble.sh. Bash will fall back to plain readline."
40	    warn "Install make (apt-get install build-essential / xcode-select --install) and re-apply."
41	    exit 0
42	fi
43	
44	# ble.sh's Makefile requires GNU awk specifically (not BSD awk on macOS,
45	# not mawk on minimal Ubuntu containers). The bash ansible role installs
46	# `gawk` on every OS; if this script runs before the role (first apply
47	# bootstrap), gracefully no-op so the rest of the apply continues.
48	if ! command -v gawk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
49	    warn "gawk not found; cannot compile ble.sh (Makefile hard-requires GNU awk)."
50	    warn "On Linux: apt-get install gawk / yum install gawk."
51	    warn "On macOS: brew install gawk."
52	    warn "Re-run \`chezmoi apply\` after gawk is available."
53	    exit 0
54	fi
55	
56	# Bash 4.0+ check — ble.sh refuses to attach on bash 3.2 (macOS system bash).
57	# On a zsh-primary mac without `brew install bash`, the bash role's brew bash
58	# install is gated on primaryShell == "bash", so the system bash 3.2 stays
59	# active. ble.sh's compile step itself works fine on 3.2 (it's a Makefile),
60	# but the runtime won't attach. We still compile so the artefact exists for
61	# any user who later switches to brew bash or runs bash 5+ ad-hoc.
62	{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
63	if [[ -x /opt/homebrew/bin/bash ]]; then
64	    BASH_BIN=/opt/homebrew/bin/bash
65	elif [[ -x /usr/local/bin/bash ]]; then
66	    BASH_BIN=/usr/local/bin/bash
67	else
68	    BASH_BIN=$(command -v bash)
69	fi
70	{{ else -}}
71	BASH_BIN=$(command -v bash)
72	{{ end -}}
73	
74	info "Compiling ble.sh (PREFIX=\$HOME/.local; bash=$BASH_BIN)"
75	if make -C "$BLESH_SRC" install PREFIX="$HOME/.local" \
76	        BASH="$BASH_BIN" \
77	        >/tmp/blesh-install.log 2>&1; then
78	    success "ble.sh installed to ~/.local/share/blesh/ble.sh"
79	    rm -f /tmp/blesh-install.log
80	else
81	    warn "ble.sh make install failed — see /tmp/blesh-install.log"
82	    warn "Bash will fall back to plain readline (bashrc presence-gate)."
83	    exit 0
84	fi
85
```

5	# Run brew bundle when Brewfiles change
6	# Runs when general Brew apps are enabled, or on macOS when AI/gaming desktop apps are enabled
7	# Profile: {{ .profile }}
8	# installBrewApps: {{ $installBrewApps }}
9	# installAiDesktopApps: {{ $installAiDesktopApps }}
10	# installGamingApps: {{ $installGamingApps }}

13	# Brew app installation is disabled for this profile
14	# To enable, run: chezmoi init --force
15	exit 0
16	{{ end -}}
17	
18	{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
19	# === Brewfile Hashes (changes trigger re-run) ===
20	# Brewfile: {{ include "dot_config/homebrew/Brewfile.tmpl" | sha256sum }}
21	# Brewfile.darwin: {{ include "dot_config/homebrew/Brewfile.darwin.tmpl" | sha256sum }}
22	{{ else -}}
23	# === Brewfile Hashes (changes trigger re-run) ===
24	# Brewfile: {{ include "dot_config/homebrew/Brewfile.tmpl" | sha256sum }}
25	# Brewfile.linux: {{ include "dot_config/homebrew/Brewfile.linux.tmpl" | sha256sum }}
26	{{ end -}}
27	
28	set -e
29	
30	# Colors
31	RED='\033[0;31m'
32	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
33	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
34	BLUE='\033[0;34m'
35	NC='\033[0m'
36	
37	info() { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
38	success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"; }
39	warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
40	error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }
41	
42	# Setup Homebrew PATH
43	{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
44	if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
45	    eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
46	elif [[ -f /usr/local/bin/brew ]]; then
47	    eval "$(/usr/local/bin/brew shellenv)"
48	fi
49	{{ else -}}
50	# Linuxbrew
51	if [[ -d /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew ]]; then
52	    eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
53	elif [[ -d "$HOME/.linuxbrew" ]]; then
54	    eval "$("$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew" shellenv)"
55	fi
56	{{ end -}}
57	
58	# Check if brew is available
59	if ! command -v brew &> /dev/null; then
60	    {{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
61	    error "Homebrew not found. It should have been installed by bootstrap."
62	    exit 1
63	    {{ else -}}
64	    warn "Homebrew (Linuxbrew) not installed. Skipping brew bundle."
65	    warn "To install: /bin/bash -c \"\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)\""
66	    exit 0
67	    {{ end -}}
68	fi
69	
70	BREWFILE_DIR="$HOME/.config/homebrew"
71	
72	# Check if Brewfiles exist
73	if [[ ! -f "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile" ]]; then
74	    warn "Brewfile not found at $BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile"
75	    warn "Run 'chezmoi apply' to deploy Brewfiles first."
76	    exit 0
77	fi
78	
79	{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
80	# --- Shared sudo session (see scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh)
81	# Inlined at render time. By the time brew-bundle runs, bootstrap and ansible
82	# will usually have already prompted + spawned the watchdog (which keeps the
83	# TTY timestamp warm every 50 s). `sudo_session_init` is idempotent: if the
84	# session already exists, it just re-exports env vars + re-registers traps.
85	# If the user invoked brew-bundle standalone (without the other phases), the
86	# prompt happens here instead.
87	{{ include "scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh" }}
88	# --- end sudo helper
89	
90	# Pre-authenticate sudo so Homebrew cask pkg installers (e.g. google-drive,
91	# squirrel, ollama-app) can invoke /usr/sbin/installer without prompting from
92	# a non-TTY child. The watchdog refreshes the timestamp every 50 s, which
93	# is well inside sudo's default 5-minute tty_tickets window.
94	if sudo_session_init "brew"; then
95	    case "$(sudo_session_skip_reason)" in
96	        cached)
97	            # Warm the current TTY's timestamp right now so the FIRST cask
98	            # that shells out to `sudo /usr/sbin/installer` finds a live
99	            # ticket (cask installers read a TTY timestamp, not stdin).
100	            sudo_session_warm_cache || warn "sudo_session_warm_cache failed; pkg casks may prompt or fail"
101	            ;;
102	        passwordless)
103	            info "Passwordless sudo detected"
104	            ;;
105	        non-interactive | "")
106	            warn "Non-interactive mode without passwordless sudo - pkg-based casks may fail"
107	            ;;
108	    esac
109	else
110	    warn "Sudo session could not be established - pkg-based casks may fail"
111	fi
112	
113	{{ end -}}
114	info "Running brew bundle..."
115	
116	# `chezmoi apply` is install-only by design (see AGENTS.md). Skip the implicit
117	# `brew update` that `brew bundle` would otherwise trigger -- it slows things
118	# down, and on hosts using a Chinese mirror it can transiently fail with
119	# "Unable to find <sha> under https://mirrors.aliyun.com/homebrew/brew.git"
120	# while the mirror catches up to upstream. Upgrades go through `just upgrade-*`.
121	export HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1
122	# Quiet "you have N outdated formulae" hints during install-only flow.
123	export HOMEBREW_NO_INSTALL_UPGRADE=1
124	
125	{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
126	# --- Adopt pre-flight (macOS only, OPT-IN)
127	# Casks installed manually (drag .app into /Applications) are invisible to brew
128	# until "adopted". `brew install --cask --adopt <name>` writes Caskroom
129	# metadata in-place so subsequent `brew bundle` runs skip the cask.
130	#
131	# Why opt-in (CHEZMOI_BREW_ADOPT_PREFLIGHT=1 to enable):
132	#
133	#   1. **Version-strict**: `--adopt` compares SHA256 of /Applications/Foo.app
134	#      against the SHA256 brew's formula expects. If your manually-installed
135	#      version differs by even a single byte (different download date, vendor
136	#      auto-update, etc.) brew raises "no installed App to adopt" and falls
137	#      back to a full download. In a typical real-world `Brewfile.darwin`
138	#      with 20+ casks, the hit rate observed was ~40% — the rest re-download
139	#      anyway, defeating the point.
140	#
141	#   2. **Password storm**: each `brew install --cask --adopt` invocation
142	#      internally calls `sudo /usr/sbin/installer` / `sudo xattr` / similar.
143	#      Our `sudo_session_warm_cache` warms the TTY timestamp but brew's
144	#      internal sudo invocations don't always reuse it (brew may `sudo -k`
145	#      before pkg installs, or use a different timestamp_type). Result:
146	#      one `Password:` prompt per pkg-based cask, often with multi-second
147	#      gaps where the user has to babysit the terminal.
148	#
149	#   3. **`--adopt` doesn't skip download**: brew calls `fetch_artifacts`
150	#      BEFORE `install_artifacts` (where --adopt branches), so casks whose
151	#      `.app` isn't present (or whose SHA mismatches) still get downloaded.
152	#      Even our filesystem pre-filter only catches the first case.
153	#
154	#   4. **Mac App Store conflicts**: if you have Tailscale (or others) from
155	#      the App Store, the brew cask version's pkg installer pops up an
156	#      interactive "already installed via Mac App Store" dialog mid-apply.
157	#      We detect this via `mas list` when opt-in is enabled.
158	#
159	# When useful:
160	#   - You're setting up a NEW mac, have just manually dragged some `.app`s
161	#     into /Applications/ from vendor DMGs, AND those apps happen to be the
162	#     CURRENT brew-formula versions (rare in practice). Most users should
163	#     leave this off and let `brew bundle` install everything fresh on
164	#     first apply — slower one-time download cost but no password storm and
165	#     no version-mismatch failures.
166	#
167	# To enable: `export CHEZMOI_BREW_ADOPT_PREFLIGHT=1` in ~/.shellrc.adhoc.
168	adopt_existing_casks() {
169	    local file="$1"
170	    [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 0
171	    command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
172	
173	    # Resolve Caskroom path once (arm64 vs intel).
174	    local caskroom=""
175	    if [[ -d /opt/homebrew/Caskroom ]]; then
176	        caskroom=/opt/homebrew/Caskroom
177	    elif [[ -d /usr/local/Caskroom ]]; then
178	        caskroom=/usr/local/Caskroom
179	    fi
180	
181	    # Snapshot /Applications/ once (lowercased, newline-separated). Avoids
182	    # forking ls/find per cask. Falls back to empty string if dir missing.
183	    local apps_lc=""
184	    if [[ -d /Applications ]]; then
185	        apps_lc="$(/bin/ls -1 /Applications 2>/dev/null | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
186	    fi
187	
188	    # Snapshot mas-installed App Store apps (lowercased). Used to skip
189	    # casks whose app the user has via App Store — adopting them would
190	    # trigger a "Mac App Store Install Detected" dialog mid-apply.
191	    # `mas list` format: "<id>  <Name>  (<version>)"
192	    local mas_lc=""
193	    if command -v mas >/dev/null 2>&1; then
194	        mas_lc="$(mas list 2>/dev/null | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
195	    fi
196	
197	    # Heuristic: does /Applications/ have an .app whose name matches this
198	    # cask? Tries a handful of candidate spellings derived from the cask
199	    # short-name. Misses on weird capitalization (chatgpt → ChatGPT.app works
200	    # because case-insensitive; aerospace → AeroSpace.app works too).
201	    _has_app_for_cask() {
202	        local short="$1"
203	        local stem="${short%-app}"
204	        stem="${stem%-desktop}"
205	        local candidates=(
206	            "${short}.app"
207	            "${stem}.app"
208	            "${short//-/ }.app"
209	            "${stem//-/ }.app"
210	            "${short//-/}.app"
211	            "${stem//-/}.app"
212	        )
213	        local c c_lc
214	        for c in "${candidates[@]}"; do
215	            c_lc="$(printf '%s' "$c" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
216	            if printf '%s\n' "$apps_lc" | grep -qFx -- "$c_lc"; then
217	                return 0
218	            fi
219	        done
220	        return 1
221	    }
222	
223	    # Heuristic: is this cask's app already installed via Mac App Store?
224	    # Matches the cask stem against `mas list` output (case-insensitive).
225	    # False positives possible (substring match) but the cost of a false
226	    # positive is just "we don't try to adopt" — cheap.
227	    _is_mas_installed() {
228	        [[ -z "$mas_lc" ]] && return 1
229	        local short="$1"
230	        local stem="${short%-app}"
231	        stem="${stem%-desktop}"
232	        # Replace dashes with spaces for the search term (App Store names
233	        # generally have spaces, e.g. "Tailscale" or "The Unarchiver").
234	        local search="${stem//-/ }"
235	        printf '%s\n' "$mas_lc" | grep -qF -- "$search" && return 0
236	        return 1
237	    }
238	
239	    local cask_list
240	    cask_list="$(brew bundle list --casks --file="$file" 2>/dev/null)" || return 0
241	    [[ -n "$cask_list" ]] || return 0
242	
243	    # Three-stage filter:
244	    #   - drop already-tracked (cheap filesystem check)
245	    #   - drop casks whose .app isn't in /Applications/ (no point — brew would
246	    #     download anyway)
247	    #   - drop casks already installed via Mac App Store (would trigger the
248	    #     "already installed via Mac App Store" pkg-installer dialog)
249	    local needs_adopt=() already=0 not_present=0 mas_conflict=0 cask short
250	    while IFS= read -r cask; do
251	        [[ -n "$cask" ]] || continue
252	        short="${cask##*/}"
253	        if [[ -n "$caskroom" && -d "$caskroom/$short" ]]; then
254	            already=$(( already + 1 ))
255	            continue
256	        fi
257	        if ! _has_app_for_cask "$short"; then
258	            not_present=$(( not_present + 1 ))
259	            continue
260	        fi
261	        if _is_mas_installed "$short"; then
262	            mas_conflict=$(( mas_conflict + 1 ))
263	            info "  skipping $short — also installed via Mac App Store (mas)"
264	            continue
265	        fi
266	        needs_adopt+=("$cask")
267	    done <<< "$cask_list"
268	
269	    local total=${#needs_adopt[@]}
270	    if (( total == 0 )); then
271	        info "Adopt pre-flight: $already tracked, $not_present not in /Applications/, $mas_conflict mas-conflict, nothing to adopt"
272	        return 0
273	    fi
274	
275	    info "Adopt pre-flight: $already tracked, $not_present not in /Applications/, $mas_conflict mas-conflict, attempting --adopt on $total cask(s)..."
276	
277	    local adopted=0 failed=0 idx=0 err
278	    local err_file
279	    err_file="$(mktemp -t cz_adopt_err.XXXXXX)"
280	    for cask in "${needs_adopt[@]}"; do
281	        idx=$(( idx + 1 ))
282	        short="${cask##*/}"
283	        info "  ($idx/$total) trying $short..."
284	        # Re-warm sudo TTY timestamp before each adopt call. Pkg-based casks
285	        # (google-drive, etc.) shell out to `sudo /usr/sbin/installer` from a
286	        # subshell that doesn't always inherit our controlling TTY. Best-effort:
287	        # brew may still re-prompt for its own sudo invocations.
288	        command -v sudo_session_warm_cache >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
289	            sudo_session_warm_cache 2>/dev/null || true
290	        if brew install --cask --adopt "$cask" >/dev/null 2>"$err_file"; then
291	            adopted=$(( adopted + 1 ))
292	            info "    ✓ adopted: $short"
293	        else
294	            failed=$(( failed + 1 ))
295	            # Surface the first non-blank brew stderr line so user can
296	            # distinguish version-mismatch from pkg-conflict from network.
297	            err="$(grep -m1 -E '^(Error|Warning):' "$err_file" 2>/dev/null \
298	                    || head -n1 "$err_file" 2>/dev/null)"
299	            if [[ -n "$err" ]]; then
300	                info "    ✗ adopt failed: $short — ${err}"
301	            else
302	                info "    ✗ adopt failed (no brew stderr captured): $short"
303	            fi
304	        fi
305	    done
306	    rm -f "$err_file"
307	
308	    info "Adopt pre-flight done: $adopted adopted, $already tracked, $not_present absent, $mas_conflict mas-conflict, $failed adopt-failed"
309	}
310	
311	# Gated on CHEZMOI_BREW_ADOPT_PREFLIGHT=1 (opt-in). See block comment above
312	# for the trade-offs that made this opt-in.
313	if [[ "${CHEZMOI_BREW_ADOPT_PREFLIGHT:-0}" = "1" ]]; then
314	    info "Adopt pre-flight: scanning Brewfile.darwin for manually-installed casks (CHEZMOI_BREW_ADOPT_PREFLIGHT=1)..."
315	    adopt_existing_casks "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile.darwin"
316	fi
317	{{ end -}}
318	
319	# Run brew bundle up to N times. Subsequent attempts only retry items that
320	# failed (already-installed packages are skipped quickly). This papers over
321	# transient GitHub CDN / HTTP/2 errors that commonly hit large cask lists.
322	# Between attempts we wipe Homebrew's `.incomplete*` lockfiles so a previously
323	# interrupted parallel `brew fetch` doesn't poison the retry with
324	# "process has already locked ... .incomplete" errors.
325	BUNDLE_MAX_ATTEMPTS="${BUNDLE_MAX_ATTEMPTS:-2}"
326	clear_brew_download_locks() {
327	    local cache_dir
328	    cache_dir="$(brew --cache 2>/dev/null)/downloads"
329	    [[ -d "$cache_dir" ]] || return 0
330	    # Both .incomplete data files and their .lock siblings.
331	    find "$cache_dir" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.incomplete*' -print -delete 2>/dev/null \
332	        | head -20 \
333	        | while IFS= read -r f; do info "  cleared stale lock: $(basename "$f")"; done
334	}
335	
336	trust_bundle_taps() {
337	    local file="$1"
338	    local taps tap info_out
339	    [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 0
340	    taps="$(brew bundle list --taps --file="$file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
341	    [[ -n "$taps" ]] || return 0
342	
343	    while IFS= read -r tap; do
344	        [[ -n "$tap" ]] || continue
345	        if ! brew tap | grep -qFx -- "$tap"; then
346	            if ! brew tap "$tap"; then
347	                warn "Could not tap $tap before brew bundle; brew bundle will retry normally"
348	                continue
349	            fi
350	        fi
351	
352	        info_out="$(brew tap-info "$tap" 2>/dev/null || true)"
353	        if [[ "$info_out" == *Untrusted* ]]; then
354	            info "Trusting Homebrew tap $tap"
355	            brew trust "$tap"
356	        fi
357	    done <<< "$taps"
358	}
359	
360	run_bundle_with_retry() {
361	    local file="$1"
362	    local label="$2"
363	    local attempt=1
364	    while (( attempt <= BUNDLE_MAX_ATTEMPTS )); do
365	        if (( attempt == 1 )); then
366	            info "Installing $label..."
367	        else
368	            info "Retrying $label (attempt $attempt/$BUNDLE_MAX_ATTEMPTS, only missing items)..."
369	            clear_brew_download_locks
370	        fi
371	        trust_bundle_taps "$file"
372	        if brew bundle --file="$file" --no-upgrade; then
373	            return 0
374	        fi
375	        attempt=$(( attempt + 1 ))
376	    done
377	    warn "Some packages in $label still failed after $BUNDLE_MAX_ATTEMPTS attempts (continuing...)"
378	    warn "Rerun later with: brew bundle --file=\"$file\" --no-upgrade"
379	    return 0
380	}
381	
382	# Run shared Brewfile first (installs mas, taps)
383	run_bundle_with_retry "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile" "shared packages from Brewfile"
384	
385	# Run platform-specific Brewfile
386	{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
387	if [[ -f "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile.darwin" ]]; then
388	    run_bundle_with_retry "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile.darwin" "macOS packages from Brewfile.darwin"
389	fi
390	
391	# Remind about mas sign-in
392	if command -v mas &> /dev/null; then
393	    if ! mas account &> /dev/null; then
394	        warn "Not signed in to Mac App Store."
395	        warn "Sign in via App Store.app to install mas apps."
396	    fi
397	fi
398	{{ else -}}
399	if [[ -f "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile.linux" ]]; then
400	    run_bundle_with_retry "$BREWFILE_DIR/Brewfile.linux" "Linux packages from Brewfile.linux"
401	fi
402	{{ end -}}
403	
404	success "Brew bundle complete!"
405
```

```text
1	# Tool managers — who installs what
2	
3	A map of every tool installed by this repo, organised by the manager that
4	owns it. Companion to [`upgrades.md`](upgrades.md) — that one is the
5	upgrade-side reference; this one is install-side.
6	
7	If you want to know:
8	
9	| Question | Section |
10	|---|---|
11	| "Where does tool X come from?" | [§ Tool index (A–Z)](#tool-index-az) |
12	| "What does manager Y install?" | [§ Per-manager catalog](#per-manager-catalog) |
13	| "I'm adding a new tool — which manager should I use?" | [§ Decision tree](#decision-tree-for-new-tools) |
14	| "Why is X managed by Z and not W?" | [§ Multi-mechanism tools + rationale](#multi-mechanism-tools--rationale) |
15	| "How is X upgraded?" | [`upgrades.md`](upgrades.md) |
16	
17	> Convention reminder: install-only by design (see [`upgrades.md` § Why
18	> install and upgrade are split](upgrades.md#why-install-and-upgrade-are-split)).
19	> Every install path here uses `state: present` / `creates:` / `[[ -d … ]]`
20	> guards; nothing in `chezmoi apply` silently bumps a tool's version. The
21	> only thing that intentionally moves tools forward is
22	> `scripts/upgrade_tools.sh` (via `just upgrade-*`).
23	
24	---
25	
26	## TL;DR — manager landscape
27	
28	| Manager | Owns | Where listed | Upgrade category |
29	|---|---|---|---|
30	| **Bootstrap** (`run_once_before_*.sh.tmpl`) | Homebrew itself, uv, mise, ansible-core, apt prereqs | `run_once_before_00_bootstrap.sh.tmpl` | n/a (one-shot, idempotent) |
31	| **Homebrew formulae** | General macOS GUI helpers (`mas`) + role-driven formulae (in `base`, `devtools`, `coding_agents`, …) | `dot_config/homebrew/Brewfile.tmpl` + scattered `community.general.homebrew:` tasks | `brew` |
32	| **Homebrew casks** | macOS GUI apps, gated by `installBrewApps`, `installAiDesktopApps`, or `installGamingApps` | `Brewfile.darwin.tmpl` | `brew` (`--cask --greedy`) |
33	| **Ansible roles** | The majority — 26 roles spanning shells, devtools, agents, language toolchains, networking, security | `dot_ansible/roles/*/tasks/main.yml` | indirect (each role calls one of: brew / apt / mise / uv / npm / cargo / gem / dotnet / curl-installer / github-release) |
34	| **mise** | Runtime versions: `node`, `bun`, `rust`, `go`, `dotnet`, `ruby` (oldEL: ruby only) | `dot_config/mise/config.toml.tmpl` | `mise` |
35	| **uv** | Python CLI tools (~13 entries in `python_uv_tools` + 1 in `llm_tools` + `litellm`) | `dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml`, `dot_ansible/roles/llm_tools/defaults/main.yml`, ad-hoc `uv tool install` in `coding_agents` (specify-cli) + `security_tools` (pre-commit) | `uv` (`uv tool upgrade --all`) |
36	| **npm** (global) | JS CLIs (~5: copilot-cli, codex, gemini-cli, openchamber, bitwarden, readability-cli) + `tldr` + `tree-sitter-cli` | `js_cli_tools/defaults/main.yml`, scattered `community.general.npm:` + `mise exec -- npm install -g` | `npm` (`npm -g update`) |
37	| **cargo** | Rust crates: `recon`, `pueue` (Linux), `tree-sitter-cli` (fallback), `alacritty` (Linux build), `modelsdev` (Linux fallback) | `rust_cargo_tools/defaults/main.yml` (currently `[]`) + hard-coded in tasks | `cargo` (`cargo install-update -a`) |
38	| **dotnet** (global tools) | `azure-cost-cli` (binary `azure-cost`) | `dotnet_tools/defaults/main.yml` | `dotnet` |
39	| **gem** | `try-cli` (binary `try`), `tmuxinator` | `ruby_gem_tools/defaults/main.yml` | `gem` |
40	| **curl-installer** (vendor `install.sh`) | Self-managed coding agents + a handful of system tools: claude, opencode, cursor-agent, agy, rtk, ollama, atuin, docker, zoxide, direnv, just, llmfit, starship | `dot_ansible/roles/coding_agents/`, `llm_tools/`, `devtools/`, `starship/`, `atuin/`, `docker/`, bootstrap | `agents` (subset of these has a known self-update subcommand) |
41	| **GitHub-release downloads** | ~30 Linux user-level fallbacks for ripgrep/fd/jq/git-lfs/gh/glab/bat/eza/git-delta/diffnav/glow/gum/vhs/freeze/yazi/superfile/btop/zellij/sesh/worktrunk/workmux/tailspin/dasel/yq/jnv/doggo/gping/trippy/bandwhich/rustscan/cloudflared/ngrok/speedtest/gitleaks/lazygit/neovim + macOS fallbacks for specstory/codexbar | Each role's `_release_fallback` block | **none** (install-only — see [Coverage gaps](#coverage-gaps-install-only-no-automated-upgrade)) |
42	| **chezmoi externals** | git checkouts on weekly refresh: oh-my-zsh + plugins, oh-my-bash, ble.sh, TPM, fzf (Linux), toolkami.rb | `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl` | `externals` (`chezmoi apply --refresh-externals`) |
43	| **apt** / **yum** | Distro packages (build deps, ffmpeg, audit, fontconfig, libnotify-bin, system git/zsh/bash, Steam launcher/runtime, …) | scattered `ansible.builtin.apt:` / `ansible.builtin.yum:` in roles | **none** (relies on `apt upgrade` outside this repo) |
44	| **flatpak** | Discord (default channel on Linux) | `gui_apps_linux/tasks/main.yml` | `flatpak` (`flatpak update -y`) |
