```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	# Initialise project-knowledge-harness in a target repo:
5	# - Create TODO.md, backlog/README.md, pitfalls/README.md from templates
6	# - Append agent guidance to AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md (auto-detect)
7	# - Append "Roadmap & lessons learned" snippet to README.md
8	# - Run todo-kanban.sh --validate-only at the end
9	#
10	# Idempotent: existing files are NEVER overwritten unless --force is given;
11	# snippets are appended only if a sentinel marker is not already present.
12	#
13	# Compatibility: macOS system Bash 3.2.
14	
15	usage() {
16	  cat <<'EOF'
17	Usage: init.sh [OPTIONS]
18	
19	Set up project-knowledge-harness files in a target project. Safe to re-run.
20	
21	Options:
22	  --target DIR              Target project root (default: current directory)
23	  --project-name NAME       Substituted into <PROJECT NAME> placeholders
24	                            (default: basename of --target)
25	  --deployment MECH         One of: chezmoi | npm | pip | docker | none
26	                            Substituted into <DEPLOYMENT MECHANISM> /
27	                            <IGNORE FILE> placeholders. Default: none.
28	  --agent-contract FILE     Path (relative to --target) to the agent
29	                            contract file to receive the guidance snippet.
30	                            Default: auto-detect AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md,
31	                            .opencode/AGENTS.md, .cursorrules. If none
32	                            exist, AGENTS.md is created.
33	  --readme FILE             Path to README.md (default: README.md in target).
34	                            Pass "" to skip the README snippet.
35	  --force                   Overwrite TODO.md / backlog/README.md /
36	                            pitfalls/README.md if they already exist.
37	  --no-validate             Skip the final todo-kanban.sh validation pass.
38	  -h, --help                Show this help and exit.
39	
40	The script never edits .gitignore / .chezmoiignore — it only prints the
41	ignore rules you should add for the chosen deployment mechanism.
42	EOF
43	}
44	
45	target="."
46	project_name=""
47	deployment="none"
48	agent_contract=""
49	readme_file=""
50	readme_explicit=0
51	force=0
52	do_validate=1
53	
54	while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
55	  case "$1" in
56	    -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
57	    --target) target="$2"; shift 2 ;;
58	    --project-name) project_name="$2"; shift 2 ;;
59	    --deployment) deployment="$2"; shift 2 ;;
60	    --agent-contract) agent_contract="$2"; shift 2 ;;
61	    --readme) readme_file="$2"; readme_explicit=1; shift 2 ;;
62	    --force) force=1; shift ;;
63	    --no-validate) do_validate=0; shift ;;
64	    *) echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 2 ;;
65	  esac
66	done
67	
68	case "$deployment" in
69	  chezmoi|npm|pip|docker|none) ;;
70	  *) echo "Error: --deployment must be chezmoi|npm|pip|docker|none" >&2; exit 2 ;;
71	esac
72	
73	target_abs="$(cd "$target" && pwd)"
74	[ -n "$project_name" ] || project_name="$(basename "$target_abs")"
75	
76	skill_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
77	assets_dir="$skill_dir/assets"
78	scripts_dir="$skill_dir/scripts"
79	
80	if [ ! -d "$assets_dir" ]; then
81	  echo "Error: assets/ not found next to scripts/ (looked in $assets_dir)" >&2
82	  exit 1
83	fi
84	
85	# Map deployment to (DEPLOYMENT MECHANISM, IGNORE FILE) labels and ignore-rule lines.
86	deployment_label=""
87	ignore_label=""
88	ignore_lines=""
89	case "$deployment" in
90	  chezmoi)
91	    deployment_label="chezmoi"
92	    ignore_label=".chezmoiignore.tmpl"
93	    ignore_lines=$'TODO.md\nbacklog/**\npitfalls/**'
94	    ;;
95	  npm)
96	    deployment_label="npm"
97	    ignore_label=".npmignore (or package.json \"files\")"
98	    ignore_lines=$'TODO.md\nbacklog/\npitfalls/'
99	    ;;
100	  pip)
101	    deployment_label="pip / setuptools"
102	    ignore_label="MANIFEST.in"
103	    ignore_lines=$'exclude TODO.md\nrecursive-exclude backlog *\nrecursive-exclude pitfalls *'
104	    ;;
105	  docker)
106	    deployment_label="Docker"
107	    ignore_label=".dockerignore"
108	    ignore_lines=$'TODO.md\nbacklog/\npitfalls/'
109	    ;;
110	  none)
111	    deployment_label="N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo)"
112	    ignore_label="N/A"
113	    ignore_lines=""
114	    ;;
115	esac
116	
117	# Sentinel markers so re-runs don't double-append snippets.
118	agent_marker="<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->"
119	readme_marker="<!-- project-knowledge-harness:readme-roadmap -->"
120	
121	# --- helper: render a template by replacing placeholders, write to stdout
122	render_template() {
123	  local src="$1"
124	  sed \
125	    -e "s|<PROJECT NAME>|${project_name}|g" \
126	    -e "s|<LINK TO PROJECT AGENT CONTRACT, e.g. AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md>|AGENTS.md|g" \
127	    -e "s|<DEPLOYMENT MECHANISM>|${deployment_label}|g" \
128	    -e "s|<DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via .chezmoiignore.tmpl>|${deployment_label} via ${ignore_label}|g" \
129	    -e "s|<DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via .chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore>|${deployment_label} via ${ignore_label}|g" \
130	    -e "s|<IGNORE FILE>|${ignore_label}|g" \
131	    "$src"
132	}
133	
134	create_file() {
135	  local rel="$1"
136	  local src="$2"
137	  local dest="$target_abs/$rel"
138	  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$dest")"
139	  if [ -e "$dest" ] && [ "$force" -eq 0 ]; then
140	    echo "skip: $rel already exists (use --force to overwrite)"
141	    return
142	  fi
143	  render_template "$src" > "$dest"
144	  echo "create: $rel"
145	}
146	
147	append_snippet() {
148	  local rel="$1"
149	  local src="$2"
150	  local marker="$3"
151	  local dest="$target_abs/$rel"
152	
153	  if [ -e "$dest" ] && grep -qF "$marker" "$dest" 2>/dev/null; then
154	    echo "skip: $rel already contains $marker"
155	    return
156	  fi
157	
158	  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$dest")"
159	  if [ ! -e "$dest" ]; then
160	    : > "$dest"
161	  fi
162	  {
163	    printf '\n%s\n' "$marker"
164	    render_template "$src"
165	    printf '%s (end)\n' "$marker"
166	  } >> "$dest"
167	  echo "append: $rel"
168	}
169	
170	detect_agent_contract() {
171	  local candidates=(AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md .opencode/AGENTS.md .cursorrules)
172	  local c
173	  for c in "${candidates[@]}"; do
174	    if [ -e "$target_abs/$c" ]; then
175	      echo "$c"
176	      return
177	    fi
178	  done
179	  echo "AGENTS.md"
180	}
181	
182	if [ -z "$agent_contract" ]; then
183	  agent_contract="$(detect_agent_contract)"
184	fi
185	
186	if [ "$readme_explicit" -eq 0 ]; then
187	  readme_file="README.md"
188	fi
189	
190	echo "Project root: $target_abs"
191	echo "Project name: $project_name"
192	echo "Deployment:   $deployment ($deployment_label, ignore in $ignore_label)"
193	echo "Agent contract: $agent_contract"
194	echo "README:       ${readme_file:-<skipped>}"
195	echo
196	
197	create_file "TODO.md" "$assets_dir/TODO.md.template"
198	create_file "backlog/README.md" "$assets_dir/backlog-README.md.template"
199	create_file "pitfalls/README.md" "$assets_dir/pitfalls-README.md.template"
200	
201	# Seed backlog/inbox.md if missing. Keep it tiny — sweep-inbox.sh handles it.
202	if [ ! -e "$target_abs/backlog/inbox.md" ]; then
203	  mkdir -p "$target_abs/backlog"
204	  cat > "$target_abs/backlog/inbox.md" <<'INBOX_EOF'
205	# Inbox
206	
207	Quick-capture area. Drop loose lines here when priority/effort/wording
208	isn't clear yet; run `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh` later to formalize them
209	into `TODO.md`.
210	
211	Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are ignored. Free-form lines
212	prompt the sweeper for missing fields; lines shaped like
213	`- priority=P3 effort=M title="X" description="Y"` (or short aliases
214	`p=`/`e=`/`t=`/`d=`) are processed automatically by `--batch`.
215	
216	<!-- inbox entries below this line; mix free-form and key=value freely -->
217	
218	INBOX_EOF
219	  echo "create: backlog/inbox.md"
220	else
221	  echo "skip: backlog/inbox.md already exists"
222	fi
223	
224	append_snippet "$agent_contract" "$assets_dir/agent-guidance.md.template" "$agent_marker"
225	if [ -n "$readme_file" ]; then
226	  append_snippet "$readme_file" "$assets_dir/readme-roadmap.md.template" "$readme_marker"
227	fi
228	
229	echo
230	if [ -n "$ignore_lines" ]; then
231	  echo "Add these lines to $ignore_label so backlog/ and pitfalls/ aren't shipped:"
232	  printf '%s\n' "$ignore_lines" | sed 's/^/  /'
233	  echo
234	fi
235	
236	if [ "$do_validate" -eq 1 ] && [ -x "$scripts_dir/todo-kanban.sh" ]; then
237	  echo "Validating TODO.md..."
238	  ( cd "$target_abs" && "$scripts_dir/todo-kanban.sh" --validate-only TODO.md )
239	fi
240	
241	cat <<'EOF'
242	
243	Next steps:
244	  1. Open TODO.md and replace the example items with real ones, or use
245	     scripts/add-todo.sh to insert structured entries:
246	       scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
247	         --title "Title" --description "Description"
248	  2. Drop loose ideas into backlog/inbox.md; formalize later via:
249	       scripts/sweep-inbox.sh             # interactive
250	       scripts/sweep-inbox.sh --batch     # only key=value lines
251	  3. Add ignore rules above to your packaging/deployment ignore file.
252	  4. Run the kanban renderer when you want a quick board view:
253	       scripts/todo-kanban.sh
254	  5. Promote shipped items via:
255	       scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"
256	EOF
257

122	      shift 2
123	      ;;
124	    --only=*)
125	      ONLY="${1#--only=}"
126	      shift
127	      ;;
128	    --skip)
129	      SKIP="$2"
130	      shift 2
131	      ;;
132	    --skip=*)
133	      SKIP="${1#--skip=}"
134	      shift
135	      ;;
136	    all)
137	      SELECTED=("${ALL_CATEGORIES[@]}")
138	      shift
139	      ;;
140	    externals | brew | mise | uv | npm | cargo | dotnet | gem | flatpak | warp | atuin | agents | plugins)
141	      SELECTED+=("$1")
142	      shift
143	      ;;
144	    *)
145	      error "Unknown argument: $1"
146	      usage
147	      exit 2
148	      ;;
149	  esac
150	done
151	
152	# No positional arg and no --only ⇒ default to 'all'
153	if [[ ${#SELECTED[@]} -eq 0 && -z "$ONLY" ]]; then
154	  SELECTED=("${ALL_CATEGORIES[@]}")
155	fi
156	
157	# --only overrides positional selection
158	if [[ -n "$ONLY" ]]; then
159	  IFS=',' read -ra SELECTED <<<"$ONLY"
160	fi
161	
162	# --skip filters the selection
163	if [[ -n "$SKIP" ]]; then
164	  IFS=',' read -ra _SKIP_ARR <<<"$SKIP"
165	  _FILTERED=()
166	  for c in "${SELECTED[@]}"; do
167	    local_skip=0
168	    for s in "${_SKIP_ARR[@]}"; do
169	      [[ "$c" == "$s" ]] && {
170	        local_skip=1
171	        break
172	      }
173	    done
174	    [[ "$local_skip" -eq 0 ]] && _FILTERED+=("$c")
175	  done
176	  SELECTED=("${_FILTERED[@]}")
177	fi
178	
179	# Validate every category exists
180	for c in "${SELECTED[@]}"; do
181	  ok=0
182	  for valid in "${ALL_CATEGORIES[@]}"; do
183	    [[ "$c" == "$valid" ]] && {
184	      ok=1
185	      break
186	    }
187	  done
188	  if [[ "$ok" -ne 1 ]]; then
189	    error "Unknown category: $c"
190	    usage
191	    exit 2
192	  fi
193	done
194	
195	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
196	# Run helper: exec a command (or dry-run it), capture rc without aborting
197	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
198	# Returns the rc of the invoked command (or 0 under --dry-run).
199	_run() {
200	  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
201	    printf '%b+ %s%b\n' "$_C_DIM" "$*" "$_C_RST"
202	    return 0
203	  fi
204	  printf '%b+ %s%b\n' "$_C_DIM" "$*" "$_C_RST"
205	  "$@"
206	}
207	
208	# _run_sh: same as _run but runs a single string through `bash -c`.
209	# Use for pipelines / redirections that _run cannot express.
210	_run_sh() {
211	  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
212	    printf '%b+ %s%b\n' "$_C_DIM" "$*" "$_C_RST"
213	    return 0
214	  fi
215	  printf '%b+ %s%b\n' "$_C_DIM" "$*" "$_C_RST"
216	  bash -c "$*"
217	}
218	
219	# run_category <name> <fn>: invoke fn, record success/fail, never abort.
220	SUCCESS_CATS=()
221	FAIL_CATS=()
222	SKIP_CATS=()
223	declare -A FAIL_REASONS
224	
225	run_category() {
226	  local name="$1" fn="$2"
227	  hr
228	  info "── category: ${name} ──"
229	  local rc=0
230	  # Disable errexit just in case someone later adds `set -e` at the top.
231	  set +e
232	  "$fn"
233	  rc=$?
234	  set +e
235	  if [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]; then
236	    success "category '$name' completed"
237	    SUCCESS_CATS+=("$name")
238	  elif [[ "$rc" -eq 77 ]]; then
239	    warn "category '$name' skipped (prerequisite missing)"
240	    SKIP_CATS+=("$name")
241	  else
242	    error "category '$name' had failures (rc=$rc)"
243	    FAIL_CATS+=("$name")
244	    FAIL_REASONS[$name]="rc=$rc"
245	  fi
246	}
247	
248	# Convenience: category functions return 77 when prerequisites absent.
249	SKIP_RC=77
250	
251	# ============================================================================
```
</details></tool-use>

475	    have_update_tool=1
476	  else

478	    if _run cargo install cargo-update; then
479	      # In dry-run, the binary won't materialise — assume success.

483	      fi

486	    fi
487	  fi
488

492	  else

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# upgrade_tools.sh — explicit, opt-in upgrade entrypoint.
3	#
4	# `chezmoi apply` is deliberately conservative (install-only, `state: present`,
5	# `creates:` idempotency). This script is the companion upgrade path: run it
6	# (or `just upgrade-*`) when you want to actually move tools forward.
7	#
8	# Design: each category is best-effort. A failure in one category does NOT
9	# abort later categories; a summary is printed at the end listing SUCCESS /
10	# FAILED / SKIPPED categories.
11	#
12	# Usage:
13	#   scripts/upgrade_tools.sh [all|<category> ...] [flags]
14	#
15	# Categories (also selectable via `all`):
16	#   externals brew mise uv npm cargo dotnet gem agents plugins
17	#
18	# Flags:
19	#   --dry-run          Print commands without executing.
20	#   --only a,b,c       Only run listed categories (comma-separated).
21	#   --skip a,b         Run everything except listed categories.
22	#   -h, --help         Show help.
23	#
24	# See docs/this_repo/upgrades.md and `## Upgrades` in AGENTS.md for rationale.
25	
26	set -u
27	# NOTE: we deliberately do not `set -e`. Each command is guarded by _run /
28	# run_category so we can continue past individual failures.
29	
30	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# Colors + loggers (match the style used in run_onchange_after_*.sh.tmpl)
32	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
34	  _C_RED='\033[0;31m'
35	  _C_GRN='\033[0;32m'
36	  _C_YLW='\033[1;33m'
37	  _C_BLU='\033[0;34m'
38	  _C_DIM='\033[2m'
39	  _C_RST='\033[0m'
40	else
41	  _C_RED='' _C_GRN='' _C_YLW='' _C_BLU='' _C_DIM='' _C_RST=''
42	fi
43	
44	info() { printf '%b\n' "${_C_BLU}[INFO]${_C_RST} $*"; }
45	success() { printf '%b\n' "${_C_GRN}[SUCCESS]${_C_RST} $*"; }
46	warn() { printf '%b\n' "${_C_YLW}[WARN]${_C_RST} $*"; }
47	error() { printf '%b\n' "${_C_RED}[ERROR]${_C_RST} $*" >&2; }
48	hr() { printf '%b\n' "${_C_DIM}────────────────────────────────────────────${_C_RST}"; }
49	
50	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	# Source shared sudo helper (same file used by run_*.sh.tmpl via `include`)
52	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	_REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
54	# shellcheck source=lib/sudo_shared.sh
55	if [[ -f "$_REPO_ROOT/scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh" ]]; then
56	  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
57	  source "$_REPO_ROOT/scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh"
58	fi
59	
60	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	# Homebrew PATH detection (mirrors .chezmoiscripts/global/run_onchange_after_30_brew_bundle.sh.tmpl)
62	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

```text
1	---
2	# Cargo CLI tools installation
3	# Rust is installed via mise
4	
5	# =============================================================================
6	# Ensure Rust is installed via mise
7	# =============================================================================
8	
9	- name: Check for user-installed mise
10	  ansible.builtin.stat:
11	    path: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.local/bin/mise"
12	  register: mise_user_install
13	
14	- name: Set mise binary path
15	  ansible.builtin.set_fact:
16	    mise_bin: "{{ mise_user_install.stat.exists | ternary(ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] + '/.local/bin/mise', '/opt/homebrew/bin/mise' if ansible_facts['os_family'] == 'Darwin' else '/usr/local/bin/mise') }}"
17	
18	# Guard on the actual cargo binary, NOT the mise installs/rust dir. mise's rust
19	# backend is rustup, which symlinks installs/rust/<ver> → ~/.cargo/bin. If
20	# ~/.cargo/bin is later wiped, the installs/rust dir lingers full of DEAD symlinks
21	# — a `creates: …/installs/rust` guard then skips the (re)install forever and the
22	# cargo shim fails with "cargo is not a valid shim". Keying on ~/.cargo/bin/cargo
23	# (same as the cargo-tools + EL7 tasks below) self-heals that state.
24	# See pitfalls/mise-rust-cargo-shim-dead.md.
25	- name: Install Rust via mise (non-EL7) — configured mirror first
26	  when: not (oldEL | default(false))
27	  ansible.builtin.command: "{{ mise_bin }} install rust@latest"
28	  args:
29	    creates: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/cargo"
30	  environment:
31	    PATH: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{{ ansible_facts['env']['PATH'] }}"
32	  register: mise_rust_install
33	  failed_when: false
34	
35	# Fallback when the user's RUSTUP_DIST_SERVER (e.g. the TUNA mirror behind the GFW)
36	# serves an obsolete/GC'd manifest for `rust@latest` — fails with "the server
37	# unexpectedly provided an obsolete version of the distribution manifest". Retry
38	# against the canonical rustup server (same reasoning as the CentOS-7 fallback
39	# below, now generalized to all platforms). crates.io stays on the user's mirror
40	# (~/.cargo/config.toml) so dependency builds keep their speed.
41	- name: Install Rust via mise (non-EL7) — official-server fallback (mirror manifest GC)
42	  when:
43	    - not (oldEL | default(false))
44	    - mise_rust_install.rc | default(0) != 0
45	  ansible.builtin.command: "{{ mise_bin }} install rust@latest"
46	  args:
47	    creates: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/cargo"
48	  environment:
49	    PATH: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{{ ansible_facts['env']['PATH'] }}"
50	    RUSTUP_DIST_SERVER: "https://static.rust-lang.org"
51	    RUSTUP_UPDATE_ROOT: "https://static.rust-lang.org/rustup"
52	
53	# CentOS 7 / RHEL 7: mise's rustup channel goes through the TUNA mirror which
54	# garbage-collects old versions; `rust@latest` 404s when TUNA hasn't pulled the
55	# newest release yet (observed 2026-05 on 1.95.0). Use upstream rustup-init
56	# directly — it respects ~/.cargo/config.toml's crates.io mirror but bypasses
57	# TUNA for the toolchain download. Path documented in
58	# dot_config/mise/config.toml.tmpl:36-37.
59	- name: Install Rust via rustup-init (CentOS/RHEL 7 fallback)
60	  when: oldEL | default(false)
61	  ansible.builtin.shell: |
62	    set -e
63	    if [ -x "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/cargo" ]; then
64	      echo "ALREADY_INSTALLED"
65	      exit 0
66	    fi
67	    curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs \
68	      | sh -s -- -y --default-toolchain stable --no-modify-path
69	  args:
70	    executable: /bin/bash
71	    creates: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/cargo"
72	  environment:
73	    RUSTUP_DIST_SERVER: "https://static.rust-lang.org"
74	    RUSTUP_UPDATE_ROOT: "https://static.rust-lang.org/rustup"
75	
76	# =============================================================================
77	# Install cargo tools (all platforms)
78	# =============================================================================
79	
80	- name: Install Cargo CLI tools via cargo install
81	  ansible.builtin.command: cargo install {{ item.name }}
82	  args:
83	    creates: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/{{ item.binary }}"
84	  loop: "{{ cargo_tools }}"
85	  loop_control:
86	    label: "{{ item.name }}"
87	  environment:
88	    PATH: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.local/share/mise/shims:{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin:{{ ansible_facts['env']['PATH'] }}"
89	
90	# =============================================================================
91	# recon — tmux-native dashboard for managing live Claude Code sessions
92	# https://github.com/gavraz/recon  (no crates.io release; install from git)
93	# Used by the `Agents` tmux popup submenu (dot_config/tmux/menu-agents.sh).
94	# Companion to `tv agent-panes` for the multi-agent overview.
95	# =============================================================================
96	
97	- name: Install recon (Claude Code tmux dashboard) via cargo install --git
98	  ansible.builtin.command: cargo install --git https://github.com/gavraz/recon --locked
99	  args:
100	    creates: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/recon"
101	  environment:
102	    PATH: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.local/share/mise/shims:{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin:{{ ansible_facts['env']['PATH'] }}"
103	
104	# =============================================================================
105	# Pueue - macOS (via Homebrew)
106	# =============================================================================
107	
108	- name: Install pueue via Homebrew (macOS)
109	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] == "Darwin"
110	  community.general.homebrew:
111	    name: pueue
112	    state: present
113	
114	# =============================================================================
115	# Pueue - Linux (via cargo + systemd)
116	# =============================================================================
117	
118	- name: Install pueue via cargo (Linux)
119	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Debian", "RedHat"]
120	  ansible.builtin.command: cargo install pueue --locked
121	  args:
122	    creates: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin/pueue"
123	  environment:
124	    PATH: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.local/share/mise/shims:{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.cargo/bin:{{ ansible_facts['env']['PATH'] }}"
125	
126	# =============================================================================
127	# Pueue version checks (warn by default, optional strict enforcement)
128	# =============================================================================
129	
130	- name: Check installed pueue version
131	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Darwin", "Debian", "RedHat"]
132	  ansible.builtin.shell: pueue --version 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}'
133	  args:
134	    executable: /bin/bash
135	  register: pueue_version_raw
136	  changed_when: false
137	  failed_when: false
138	
139	- name: Check installed pueued version
140	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Darwin", "Debian", "RedHat"]
141	  ansible.builtin.shell: pueued --version 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}'
142	  args:
143	    executable: /bin/bash
144	  register: pueued_version_raw
145	  changed_when: false
146	  failed_when: false
147	
148	- name: Parse installed pueue versions
149	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Darwin", "Debian", "RedHat"]
150	  ansible.builtin.set_fact:
151	    pueue_version_installed: "{{ (pueue_version_raw.stdout | trim | regex_search('[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+')) | default('0.0.0', true) }}"
152	    pueued_version_installed: "{{ (pueued_version_raw.stdout | trim | regex_search('[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+')) | default('0.0.0', true) }}"
153	
154	- name: Display installed pueue versions
155	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Darwin", "Debian", "RedHat"]
156	  ansible.builtin.debug:
157	    msg: "Installed pueue versions: pueue={{ pueue_version_installed }}, pueued={{ pueued_version_installed }} (required: >= {{ pueue_min_version }}, enforce={{ pueue_version_enforce | bool }})"
158	
159	- name: Warn if pueue versions are below minimum (non-enforcing mode)
160	  when:
161	    - ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Darwin", "Debian"]
162	    - not (pueue_version_enforce | bool)
163	    - pueue_version_installed is version(pueue_min_version, '<') or pueued_version_installed is version(pueue_min_version, '<')
164	  ansible.builtin.debug:
165	    msg: "WARNING: pueue={{ pueue_version_installed }}, pueued={{ pueued_version_installed }} are below required {{ pueue_min_version }}. Set pueue_version_enforce=true to fail on this condition."
166	
167	- name: Enforce minimum pueue versions
168	  when:
169	    - ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Darwin", "Debian"]
170	    - pueue_version_enforce | bool
171	  ansible.builtin.assert:
172	    that:
173	      - pueue_version_installed is version(pueue_min_version, '>=')
174	      - pueued_version_installed is version(pueue_min_version, '>=')
175	    fail_msg: "pueue version requirement failed: required >= {{ pueue_min_version }}, found pueue={{ pueue_version_installed }}, pueued={{ pueued_version_installed }}"
176	    success_msg: "pueue version requirement satisfied: pueue={{ pueue_version_installed }}, pueued={{ pueued_version_installed }}"
177	
178	- name: Create systemd user directory
179	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Debian", "RedHat"]
180	  ansible.builtin.file:
181	    path: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.config/systemd/user"
182	    state: directory
183	    mode: '0755'
184	
185	- name: Install pueued systemd user service
186	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Debian", "RedHat"]
187	  ansible.builtin.copy:
188	    dest: "{{ ansible_facts['env']['HOME'] }}/.config/systemd/user/pueued.service"
189	    mode: '0644'
190	    content: |
191	      [Unit]
192	      Description=Pueue Daemon - CLI Process Queue
193	      Documentation=https://github.com/Nukesor/pueue
194	
195	      [Service]
196	      ExecStart=%h/.cargo/bin/pueued --verbose
197	      Restart=on-failure
198	      RestartSec=5
199	
200	      [Install]
201	      WantedBy=default.target
202	
203	- name: Enable and start pueued service
204	  when: ansible_facts["os_family"] in ["Debian", "RedHat"]
205	  ansible.builtin.systemd:
206	    name: pueued
207	    scope: user
208	    enabled: true
209	    state: started
210	    daemon_reload: true
211	  ignore_errors: true  # Fails when no user systemd instance is reachable:
212	                       #   - containers without systemd (PID 1 != systemd)
213	                       #   - SSH session before `loginctl enable-linger` took effect
214	                       #   - CentOS 7 / RHEL 7: stock systemd 219 has NO user@.service
215	                       #     template at all, so even with linger enabled there is no
216	                       #     user instance to talk to. See
217	                       #     pitfalls/centos7-systemd-user-instance-missing.md for the
218	                       #     full failure mode + manual workaround (run pueued under
219	                       #     tmux/nohup, or install as a system service with User=).
220	                       # The unit file we wrote above stays on disk regardless,
221	                       # ready for any of the manual paths.
222
```

```text
1	# Tag schema (priority × effort)
2	
3	Two orthogonal axes prevent the "important but unimplementable" trap:
4	
5	## Priority
6	
7	- `P1` — likely next batch (you'd reach for this if you sat down today)
8	- `P2` — worth doing, no rush
9	- `P3` — someday / nice-to-have
10	- `P?` — needs evaluation first; spike before committing to a priority
11	
12	## Effort
13	
14	- `S` — under an hour
15	- `M` — half day
16	- `L` — multi-day
17	- `XL` — architectural; design doc required before code
18	
19	## Useful combinations
20	
21	- `P?` + `[?/L]` — explicit "unknown of size L"; the most honest tag
22	- `P3` + `[S]` — "small enough to slip into any free moment"
23	- `P1` + `[XL]` — warns "you said this is urgent but it's actually huge — re-scope"
24	
25	## Heading and item syntax (validator-checked)
26	
27	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` enforces this exact form. If you change
28	the syntax, update the script in the same commit.
29	
30	- Section headings, in order: `## P1`, `## P2`, `## P3`, `## P?`, `## Done`
31	- Active items in `P1` / `P2` / `P3`:
32	  `- [ ] **[Effort] Title** — description`
33	- Active items in `P?`:
34	  `- [ ] **[?/Effort] Title** — description`
35	- Shipped items in `Done`:
36	  `- ✅ [YYYY-MM-DD] [P#/Effort] Title — one-line shipped summary`
37	- Optional trailing on active items:
38	  `→ [research](backlog/<slug>.md)`
39	
40	Anything that is NOT a top-level `- [ ]` / `- ✅` item — prose paragraphs,
41	blockquotes, HTML comments, `---` rules, indented sub-bullets — is ignored
42	by the validator. Use this room for explanatory text without breaking the
43	machine-readability of the lanes.
44
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1:# Upgrades — Explicit, Opt-in Tool Refresh
7:## Why install and upgrade are split
22:## Entry points
27:just upgrade-<category>   # run one category in isolation
41:The script runs categories in the canonical `ALL_CATEGORIES` order regardless of CLI arg order — dependencies between categories are real (see [§ Run order](#run-order)).
43:## Category matrix
45:| Category | What actually happens |
56:| `warp` | **Linux only.** `sudo apt-get update` + `sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade -y warp-terminal` via the shared sudo session. macOS Warp is handled by `cat_brew` (cask `warp` + `--greedy`); this category short-circuits with `SKIPPED` on Darwin. The on-disk binary is replaced but the running Warp process is **not** restarted — quit + relaunch Warp to load the new version. The in-app `warp_finish_update <token>` graceful-restart only works from inside a live Warp session (Warp-injected shell function, not on `$PATH` otherwise) — see [`docs/tools/warp.md`](../tools/warp.md) for the full mechanism. |
61:### Run order
84:## Semantics: best-effort, not all-or-nothing
86:Each category is wrapped in a `run_category` helper:
88:- Individual command failures inside a category do **not** abort the rest of the category. Everything is best-effort.
89:- A category failure does **not** abort the next category. The script keeps going.
101:Overall process exit code is `1` iff at least one category is in FAILED. `--dry-run` always exits `0` because no real command runs.
103:## Sample: `just upgrade-dry-run`
110:[INFO] ── category: externals ──
115:[SUCCESS] category 'externals' completed
117:[INFO] ── category: brew ──
124:[SUCCESS] category 'brew' completed
128:## Things intentionally excluded
132:- **No Linux Steam upgrade category.** The Ubuntu Desktop Steam launcher/runtime packages are apt-managed through Valve's repo, so they move with your normal system apt upgrade cadence; the Steam client itself self-updates on launch. macOS Steam is a Homebrew cask and is covered by `just upgrade-brew`.
137:## Troubleshooting
141:- **`uv self update` no-ops on Homebrew uv.** Handled automatically — `cat_uv()` detects the install style and runs `brew upgrade uv` instead. 151:2. **New `curl | bash` installer.** Add a guarded block inside `cat_agents()` in [`scripts/upgrade_tools.sh`](../../scripts/upgrade_tools.sh). Gate on the binary being present so the upgrade path never bootstraps on machines that don't have the tool.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Show ALL_CATEGORIES array and dispatch tail of upgrade_tools.sh

`grep -nE "ALL_CATEGORIES=" /Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/scripts/upgrade_tools.sh; sed -n '884,939p' /Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/scripts/upgrade_tools.sh`

```text
90:ALL_CATEGORIES=(externals brew mise uv npm cargo dotnet gem flatpak warp atuin agents plugins)
# ============================================================================
# Main: dispatch selected categories in defined order
# ============================================================================
hr
if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
  info "upgrade_tools.sh — categories: ${SELECTED[*]} (dry-run)"
else
  info "upgrade_tools.sh — categories: ${SELECTED[*]}"
fi
if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
  warn "DRY-RUN MODE — commands are printed, not executed"
fi

# Run in the canonical ALL_CATEGORIES order (regardless of CLI arg order).
for cat in "${ALL_CATEGORIES[@]}"; do
  for sel in "${SELECTED[@]}"; do
    if [[ "$cat" == "$sel" ]]; then
      case "$cat" in
        externals) run_category externals cat_externals ;;
        brew) run_category brew cat_brew ;;
        mise) run_category mise cat_mise ;;
        uv) run_category uv cat_uv ;;
        npm) run_category npm cat_npm ;;
        cargo) run_category cargo cat_cargo ;;
        dotnet) run_category dotnet cat_dotnet ;;
        gem) run_category gem cat_gem ;;
        flatpak) run_category flatpak cat_flatpak ;;
        warp) run_category warp cat_warp ;;
        atuin) run_category atuin cat_atuin ;;
        agents) run_category agents cat_agents ;;
        plugins) run_category plugins cat_plugins ;;
      esac
      break
    fi
  done
done

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
hr
info "Upgrade Summary"
if [[ ${#SUCCESS_CATS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
  success "OK:      ${SUCCESS_CATS[*]}"
fi
if [[ ${#SKIP_CATS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
  warn "SKIPPED: ${SKIP_CATS[*]} (prerequisite missing)"
fi
if [[ ${#FAIL_CATS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
  error "FAILED:  ${FAIL_CATS[*]}"
  for c in "${FAIL_CATS[@]}"; do
    error "   - ${c}: ${FAIL_REASONS[$c]:-unknown}"
  done
  exit 1
fi
exit 0
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/this_repo/upgrades.md`

```text
43	## Category matrix
44	
45	| Category | What actually happens |
46	| --- | --- |
47	| `externals` | `chezmoi upgrade` (the chezmoi binary itself) + `chezmoi apply --refresh-externals` (force-refresh the 168h externals: oh-my-zsh, TPM, toolkami.rb, fzf). First because a chezmoi version bump may change how later steps behave. |
48	| `brew` | `brew update` → `brew upgrade` → `brew upgrade --cask --greedy` → `brew bundle --file=~/.config/homebrew/Brewfile` *without* `--no-upgrade` → `Brewfile.{darwin,linux}` → `brew cleanup`. macOS pre-warms the shared sudo session via [`scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh`](../../scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh) so cask pkg installers that shell out to `sudo /usr/sbin/installer` find a live ticket. |
49	| `mise` | `mise self-update --yes` + `mise upgrade` (honours the version constraints in `~/.config/mise/config.toml`). `self-update` warns, rather than fails, when mise was installed via brew/apt. |
50	| `uv` | Detects install style by binary path (Homebrew vs curl-installer) and dispatches to the right channel: `brew upgrade uv` for Homebrew/Linuxbrew installs, `uv self update` for the standalone curl installer. Then `uv tool upgrade --all`. Covers every tool listed in [`python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml`](../../dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml) and [`llm_tools/defaults/main.yml`](../../dot_ansible/roles/llm_tools/defaults/main.yml). The `python_uv_tools` ansible role does the same dispatch automatically when uv is below `min_uv_version` — see [`docs/this_repo/uv-bootstrap.md`](uv-bootstrap.md). |
51	| `npm` | `npm -g update`, falling back to `mise exec -- npm -g update` when `npm` is not directly on PATH (same detection as [`js_cli_tools`](../../dot_ansible/roles/js_cli_tools/tasks/main.yml) / [`bitwarden`](../../dot_ansible/roles/bitwarden/tasks/main.yml)). |
52	| `cargo` | If absent, bootstraps the `cargo-update` crate, then `cargo install-update -a`. Covers pueue (Linux) plus any future entries in [`rust_cargo_tools/defaults/main.yml`](../../dot_ansible/roles/rust_cargo_tools/defaults/main.yml). |
53	| `dotnet` | Parses tool names from [`dotnet_tools/defaults/main.yml`](../../dot_ansible/roles/dotnet_tools/defaults/main.yml) and runs `dotnet tool update --global <name>` per tool (via the mise dotnet shim). Falls back to `dotnet tool list --global` if parsing finds nothing. |
54	| `gem` | `gem update --system` + `gem update` via the mise ruby shim. |
55	| `flatpak` | `flatpak update --user --noninteractive --assumeyes` for user-scope Flathub apps (Discord et al. when `discordChannel=flatpak` — see [`docs/playbooks/linux-gui-apps.md`](../playbooks/linux-gui-apps.md)). Skipped when `flatpak` is absent OR when no user-scope apps are installed. System-scope (`flatpak update --system`) is intentionally NOT covered — it requires `sudo` and is rare in this repo's flow; run manually if needed. |
56	| `warp` | **Linux only.** `sudo apt-get update` + `sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade -y warp-terminal` via the shared sudo session. macOS Warp is handled by `cat_brew` (cask `warp` + `--greedy`); this category short-circuits with `SKIPPED` on Darwin. The on-disk binary is replaced but the running Warp process is **not** restarted — quit + relaunch Warp to load the new version. The in-app `warp_finish_update <token>` graceful-restart only works from inside a live Warp session (Warp-injected shell function, not on `$PATH` otherwise) — see [`docs/tools/warp.md`](../tools/warp.md) for the full mechanism. |
57	| `agents` | Re-runs the official `curl \| bash` installers for **tools already present** only — Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor CLI, Ollama (Linux), llmfit (Linux), RTK. Bootstrap list mirrors [`coding_agents`](../../dot_ansible/roles/coding_agents/tasks/main.yml). |
58	| `atuin` | macOS: `brew upgrade atuin` (no-op when atuin was installed via the upstream installer rather than brew). Linux: re-runs `curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf https://setup.atuin.sh \| env ATUIN_NO_MODIFY_PATH=1 sh` so the upstream installer drops the new binary into `~/.atuin/bin/` while leaving PATH wiring to [`dot_config/shell/15_atuin.sh`](../../dot_config/shell/15_atuin.sh). Skipped with `SKIPPED` if `atuin` is not on PATH. The local SQLite history (`~/.local/share/atuin/`) and config (`~/.config/atuin/config.toml`) are untouched. |
59	| `plugins` | `nvim --headless "+Lazy! sync" +qa` → `~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/update_plugins all` → refresh installed `claude-hud` via [`claude_hud_sync.py`](../../dot_ansible/roles/coding_agents/files/claude_hud_sync.py) → `pre-commit autoupdate` (on the dotfiles repo root) → `tldr --update` → `gh extension upgrade --all`. Each step is guarded on the relevant binary being present. |
60	
61	### Run order
62	
63	```mermaid
64	flowchart LR
65	    externals["externals<br/>(chezmoi + externals)"] --> brew
66	    brew["brew<br/>(formulas + casks greedy + Brewfile)"] --> mise
67	    mise["mise<br/>(self-update + runtimes)"] --> uv
68	    uv["uv<br/>(self update + tools)"] --> npm
69	    npm["npm<br/>(-g update)"] --> cargo
70	    cargo["cargo<br/>(install-update -a)"] --> dotnet
71	    dotnet["dotnet<br/>(tool update --global)"] --> gem
72	    gem["gem<br/>(gem update)"] --> flatpak
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/this_repo/upgrades.md`

```text
146	## Extending
147	
148	Three ways to add a new tool to the upgrade flow:
149	
150	1. **Tool already managed by an existing category (brew / uv / npm / cargo / dotnet / gem / mise).** Nothing to do — the generic `upgrade-<category>` picks it up because it uses the package manager's own bulk command.
151	2. **New `curl | bash` installer.** Add a guarded block inside `cat_agents()` in [`scripts/upgrade_tools.sh`](../../scripts/upgrade_tools.sh). Gate on the binary being present so the upgrade path never bootstraps on machines that don't have the tool.
152	
153	   ```bash
154	   if command -v newtool >/dev/null 2>&1; then
155	     info "Upgrading newtool"
156	     _run_sh "curl -fsSL https://newtool.dev/install.sh | bash" || any_fail=1
157	     ran_any=1
158	   fi
159	   ```
160	
161	3. **Brand-new strategy (e.g. GitHub release binary with custom download logic).** Add a `cat_<name>` function, register it in:
162	   - `ALL_CATEGORIES` array
163	   - The dispatch `case` in the main loop
164	   - A matching `just upgrade-<name>` recipe in [`justfile`](../../justfile)
165	   - This doc's [category matrix](#category-matrix)
166	
167	Keep the return-value contract: `0` = success, `77` = skipped (prerequisite missing), any other non-zero = failure.
168	
169	## Cross-references
170	
171	- [`AGENTS.md` § Hard repo invariants](../../AGENTS.md#install-vs-upgrade-is-split-on-purpose) — short version (install-vs-upgrade rule) written for AI agents touching this repo.
172	- [`README.md` § Keeping tools up-to-date](../../README.md) — short version written for humans arriving from the front page.
173	- [`scripts/upgrade_tools.sh`](../../scripts/upgrade_tools.sh) — the implementation.
174	- [`scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh`](../../scripts/lib/sudo_shared.sh) — the sudo-session helper reused for macOS cask installers.
175	- [`.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl`](../../.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl) — the `refreshPeriod` entries that `upgrade-externals` force-refreshes.
176	- Ansible role defaults consumed for enumeration: [`dotnet_tools`](../../dot_ansible/roles/dotnet_tools/defaults/main.yml), [`python_uv_tools`](../../dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml), [`llm_tools`](../../dot_ansible/roles/llm_tools/defaults/main.yml), [`coding_agents`](../../dot_ansible/roles/coding_agents/tasks/main.yml).
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
9	.chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore> — it is repo
10	metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64	
65	## Symptom
66	
67	Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).
68	
69	## Root cause
70	
71	Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.
72	
73	## Workaround
74	
75	Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.
76	
77	## Prevention
78	
79	How to avoid stepping on this again.
80	
81	## Related
82	
83	Links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues.
84	```
85	
86	## Index
87	
88	Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.
89	
90	| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| (none yet) | | |
93	
94	## Cross-referenced pitfalls (still in their original homes)
95	
96	These traps are documented elsewhere and aren't duplicated here — the table
97	exists so grepping `pitfalls/` still finds them. Move into this folder only
98	if their original location stops being a natural reading flow.
99	
100	| Trap | Lives in | Why not here |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| (example: Tool X version Y bug) | `docs/tool-x.md` → "Known issues" | Already part of the tool's normal config narrative |
103
```
</details></tool-use>

# TODO

Long-term backlog for translate. See AGENTS.md
for the maintenance workflow that agents should follow.

> **For agents**: when the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being
> implemented this session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have",
> "工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add it here with priority + effort tags.
> Do not create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files —
> `TODO.md` is the single backlog index. Long-form research goes in
> [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/).

<!-- Use the exact section order: P1, P2, P3, P?, Done.
     The bundled scripts/todo-kanban.sh validator only inspects top-level
     `- [ ]` and `- ✅` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
     ignored — feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
     machine readability. -->

## P1

Likely next batch — items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.

## P2

Worth doing, no rush.

- [ ] **[S] Align `just install` with `go install` / `~/.local/bin`** — the `install` recipe copies the binary to `~/.dotfiles/bin` (PATH position 11), which **shadows** the `go install` location `~/.local/bin` (position 13); a stale `just install` copy would win silently. Point the recipe at `GOBIN=$HOME/.local/bin go install .` (or drop it) so both paths agree. See [pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md](pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md).

## P3

Someday / nice-to-have.

- [ ] **[M] lingua-go detection upgrade behind the `Detector` seam** — whatlanggo is light/fast but weak on short and mixed-script text; lingua-go is more accurate but heavy (embedded n-gram models → bigger binary, slower init). Swap behind the existing `internal/lang` interface only if short-text detection proves insufficient. Detection is mostly a fallback (Google returns the source, LLM returns `DetectedSource`), so keeping it light is defensible.
- [ ] **[S] MyMemory fallback engine** — trivial flat JSON (`responseData.translatedText`, `matches[]`); wire as an easy secondary free path after Google. Limits: 5k chars/day anon (50k with `&de=email`), 500 B max per `q`, no source auto-detect.
- [ ] **[M] Azure Translator engine behind the engine seam** — the only supported Microsoft path (needs an Azure subscription key). Keyless Bing scraping (`ttranslatev3` + transient `IG`/`IID`/token) is too fragile/ToS-risky for v1. Leave the engine interface ready; add when a key is available.

## P?

Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.

- [ ] **[?/L] Ship prebuilt release binaries (goreleaser + GitHub Releases)** — cross-compile per OS/arch (pure-Go, no cgo — trivial) so hosts without a Go toolchain install via chezmoi `.chezmoiexternal` with a templated `{{ .chezmoi.os }}/{{ .chezmoi.arch }}` URL instead of `go install`. Also unlocks shipping the dictionary DB as a release asset. → [research](backlog/release-binaries.md)
- [ ] **[?/M] Bundle or prebuild the dictionary vs the 67 MB runtime `dict update`** — evaluate embedding a trimmed DB via `go:embed`, or shipping the built ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT as release assets, so first run isn't a big download/build. Weigh binary-size blowup vs first-run friction. → [research](backlog/dict-bundling.md)

## Done

Recently shipped. When implementing an active item, in the same commit run:

\```
scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<one-line shipped summary>"
\```

This moves the entry here using the dated `Done` syntax and re-validates.

- ✅ [2026-07-10] [P1/M] Publish as a public repo + `go install` — renamed the module `translate` → `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, rewrote 22 internal imports, tagged `v0.1.0`, and confirmed `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest` installs into `~/.local/bin` (GOBIN-pinned, XDG-clean, stable across mise Go upgrades).

<!-- Prune older entries into CHANGELOG.md once prior-year items appear here
     or this section grows past ~20 entries. -->

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 17:41:21Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm pulling together the report now. The key findings show a cross-file maintenance rules table that tracks how different installation mechanisms—like Ansible roles, Brewfile, curl installers, mise, uv, npm, cargo, gem, and dotnet—require corresponding updates to the tool management documentation when tools are installed, removed, or switched.

When a new tool arrives through an existing mechanism, I need to add or update its row in the tool index and potentially update the per-manager catalog and decision tree sections. The documentation also needs to stay in sync with upgrades.md if the tool has a non-trivial upgrade story. For new documentation files, I'm checking that mkdocs.yml gets the nav entry and the build passes strict validation. If the tool surfaces new capabilities to agents—like a new prompt key or executable CLI—then the SKILL.md template needs updating, though self-discovering tools like translate might not require changes unless they introduce a new prompt key. Completion generation only applies if the upstream tool ships with completions.

There's a hard rule that install and upgrade paths are intentionally split: chezmoi apply and ansible handle installation with state present, while upgrade_tools.sh and just commands manage updates. For a go install tool that doesn't need sudo, there's no sudo surface to add. Looking at the decision tree, there's no dedicated go-install branch, but the pattern across language toolchains is clear—they all live in dedicated ansible roles under dot_ansible/roles with a defaults list and tasks loop, get registered in the playbooks, tracked in the run_onchange script, and upgraded through a cat_* category.

Go should follow this exact pattern: a new `go_tools` ansible role mirroring `rust_cargo_tools`. Since go's runtime is already managed by mise (go = "latest"), the role would resolve go via mise shims and set GOBIN=~/.local/bin before running go install commands. The run_onchange script approach isn't how language package managers are handled here—those are all ansible roles. Run_onchange is reserved for ansible triggers, brew bundle, themes, and compilation tasks. So a dedicated `go_tools` role is the idiomatic answer, directly parallel to how cargo tools are organized.

For the run_onchange pattern itself, I'm looking at the bash shebang setup with a hash-trigger comment that pipes template includes to sha256sum so the script re-fires when content changes. There's OS and feature-flag gating using chezmoi conditionals, plus color helpers and prereq guards that exit gracefully with a warning rather than failing hard if a tool isn't found.

Examining the upgrade story...

the scripts/upgrade_tools.sh file dispatches across categories defined in ALL_CATEGORIES, with each category having its own function like cat_cargo and cat_npm that handle bulk updates. The return contract is 0 for success, 77 for skipped, anything else signals failure. For go, I'd mirror the cargo pattern by creating a cat_go function that resolves the go binary via mise, then iterates through the go_tools list and runs go install with @latest for each one since go doesn't have a bulk update command like cargo does.

Looking at the existing script naming in .chezmoiscripts/global/, there's a clear numbering scheme: run_once_before_10 for migrations, then run_onchange_after_20/25/26/30/40 for various setup tasks, and run_after_50/99 for post-install steps.

If go tools get added as an ansible role, no new chezmoi script is needed—the existing run_onchange_after_20_ansible_roles.sh.tmpl just picks it up with a hash line. But if going the standalone script route instead, I'd number it around 35 (after brew at 30, before skills at 40) since go runtime comes from mise in the bootstrap phase anyway. The ansible role approach is cleaner though, requiring no new script at all.

For GOBIN, the user wants to set it to ~/.local/bin to match the repo's blessed convention for auto-installed tools (where cargo, uv, npm already land). Currently GOPATH defaults to ~/go and go install puts binaries in ~/go/bin, which is on PATH via 02_legacy_tools.sh, but ~/.local/bin is the canonical location. Setting GOBIN=~/.local/bin in the install mechanism is a new, consistent choice with no conflicts elsewhere.

On SKILL.md.tmpl: since `translate` is a third-party CLI without a new prompt key, editing the template isn't required—the skill self-discovers via its docs. However, if the tool ships completions, I need to add a regen row to scripts/generate_completions.sh. Also, since go tools depend on the go runtime (which is gated on installExtraRuntimes), a go_tools role should either gate on that flag or use the herdr-plus pattern that gracefully no-ops if go isn't found. Let me verify the run_onchange_after_40_install_global_skills header to confirm the pattern.

Now I'm ready to write the final structured report with exact file paths, line numbers, and concrete snippets. For Part 2, the answer is clear: a new dedicated `go_tools` ansible role (mirroring rust_cargo_tools), registered in both playbooks and hash-tracked in run_onchange_after_20, with a defaults/main.yml list and mise-resolved go + GOBIN setup. This is the idiomatic approach because go install is a new language package manager category, and every existing language package manager gets its own *_tools role.
</details></think>

# Ship prebuilt release binaries (goreleaser + GitHub Releases)

**Status**: P? — needs a spike
**Effort**: L
**Related**: `TODO.md` P? · `.goreleaser.yaml` (none yet) · chezmoi `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl` in the dotfiles repo · [dict-bundling.md](dict-bundling.md)

## Context

2026-07, surfaced while making translate a "resident binary" in the chezmoi
dotfiles. Chosen install path for now is **`go install
github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest`** into `~/.local/bin` (GOBIN-pinned).
That requires a Go toolchain on every host. The dotfiles fleet is multi-host /
multi-arch (this host is `macos_intel`/amd64; others differ). Prebuilt release
binaries would let hosts **without** Go install via chezmoi `.chezmoiexternal`
(archive-file, templated per OS/arch) — the same mechanism the dotfiles already
use for oh-my-zsh etc.

## Investigation

- Binary is **pure Go, no cgo** (`modernc.org/sqlite` is pure Go), so
  `GOOS`/`GOARCH` cross-compilation is trivial — a single goreleaser run can emit
  darwin/{amd64,arm64} + linux/{amd64,arm64} archives.
- Current binary size ~22 MB (dictionary is NOT embedded — see dict-bundling).
- chezmoi external pattern (from the dotfiles `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl`):
  `type = "archive-file"` with a URL templated on `{{ .chezmoi.os }}` /
  `{{ .chezmoi.arch }}`, `refreshPeriod`, extract the single binary to
  `~/.local/bin`.

## Options considered

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| A. `go install @version` (current) | zero release infra; always builds for the host | needs Go toolchain per host; version pin is a git tag |
| B. goreleaser + Releases + `.chezmoiexternal` archive | no toolchain on target; multi-arch; can attach dict DB | release pipeline to maintain; must tag + run CI on each version |
| C. Homebrew tap | `brew`-native upgrade story | tap repo to maintain; macOS/Linuxbrew only |

## Current blocker / open questions

- Is `go install` "good enough"? Go is present on all current hosts (mise), so B
  is only needed if a Go-less host joins the fleet.
- If we do B, should the dictionary DB ship as a release asset (ties this doc to
  [dict-bundling.md](dict-bundling.md))?

## Decision (if any)

`2026-07 deferred` — `go install` covers all current hosts. Revisit B if a
Go-less host needs translate, or once versioned releases are wanted for their own
sake.

## References

- goreleaser: https://goreleaser.com/
- chezmoi externals: https://www.chezmoi.io/reference/special-files-and-directories/chezmoiexternal-format/

# Bundle or prebuild the dictionary vs the 67 MB runtime `dict update`

**Status**: P? — needs a spike
**Effort**: M
**Related**: `TODO.md` P? · `cmd/dictupdate.go` · `internal/engine/dictdata.go` · `internal/engine/localdict.go` · [release-binaries.md](release-binaries.md)

## Context

2026-07. The offline bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT zh→en + ECDICT en→zh) is
**not** shipped with the binary. First use requires `translate dict update all`,
a one-time ~67 MB download + ECDICT sqlite build (the build "takes a minute").
Until then, Chinese lookups prompt to run the update and English lookups fall
back to dictionaryapi.dev. This is friction on a fresh machine, and it's
per-machine (data lands in `~/.local/share/translate/dict`).

## Investigation

- Acquisition today: `cmd/dictupdate.go` → `engine.DownloadCedict` (gz text) and
  `engine.BuildEcdictDB` (downloads ECDICT, builds a local sqlite via
  `modernc.org/sqlite`).
- The code comment marks explicit `dict update` as "the blessed path"
  (`AutoDownload: false`), deliberately not auto-downloading.
- Licensing: CC-CEDICT is **CC BY-SA 4.0** (© MDBG / Paul Denisowski); ECDICT is
  **MIT** (© skywind3000). Redistribution is allowed with attribution — so
  bundling/shipping is legally fine if attribution is preserved (already in
  README).

## Options considered

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| A. Runtime `dict update` (current) | tiny binary; user opts in; always fresh | 67 MB first-run download + minute-long build; per-machine |
| B. `go:embed` a trimmed/compressed DB | offline out of the box; one binary | binary balloons (tens of MB); rebuild to refresh data; embeds a snapshot |
| C. Ship prebuilt DB as a release asset | no per-host build; smaller than embed-all | needs the release pipeline ([release-binaries.md](release-binaries.md)); still a download |
| D. Prebuild sqlite in CI, `go:embed` only that | skips the slow local build step | still big; still a snapshot |

## Current blocker / open questions

- How big is a *trimmed* DB (common headwords only) vs full? Full ECDICT sqlite is
  the heavy part — measure before deciding embed vs asset.
- Is offline-on-first-run actually wanted, or is the API fallback + opt-in update
  the right default? (The author deliberately chose opt-in.)
- Option C couples to the release-binaries spike; do that first.

## Decision (if any)

`2026-07 deferred` — keep opt-in `dict update`. Reconsider embedding/shipping a
prebuilt DB if first-run friction becomes a real complaint, and prefer measuring
a trimmed-DB size before committing to `go:embed`.

## References

- CC-CEDICT: https://www.mdbg.net/chinese/dictionary?page=cedict
- ECDICT: https://github.com/skywind3000/ECDICT
- `go:embed`: https://pkg.go.dev/embed

```

```md
# go install fails: "module declares its path as: translate" but required as github.com/...

**Symptoms** (grep this section): `go install` error `module declares its
path as: translate`, `but was required as: github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`,
`parsing go.mod`; `go install github.com/...@latest` refuses to install a repo
whose `go.mod` says `module translate`.
**First seen**: 2026-07
**Affects**: any Go module whose `module` path in `go.mod` doesn't match its
import/repo URL; `go install <path>@<ver>`; Go 1.16+ (module-aware install).
**Status**: fixed — module renamed to the GitHub path.

## Symptom

\```
$ go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest
go: github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@v0.1.0: parsing go.mod:
	module declares its path as: translate
	        but was required as: github.com/daviddwlee84/translate
\```

The project built fine locally (`go build .`) with `module translate` in
`go.mod` and imports like `"translate/internal/config"`. It only breaks when you
try to `go install` it by its resolvable URL, because the declared module path
and the requested path disagree.

## Root cause

`go install <pkg>@<version>` fetches the module and requires that the path in the
downloaded `go.mod` **exactly equals** the path you asked for. A local-only module
name (`module translate`) is legal for `go build` in the working tree but is not a
resolvable import path — `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest`
downloads the repo, reads `module translate`, and rejects the mismatch. This is Go
enforcing that a module's identity is its import path.

## Workaround

Rename the module to the repo URL and rewrite every internal import prefix in the
same pass, then rebuild:

\```sh
go mod edit -module github.com/daviddwlee84/translate
# rewrite internal imports "translate/..." -> "github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/..."
grep -rl '"translate/' --include='*.go' . \
  | xargs sed -i '' 's#"translate/#"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/#g'   # macOS BSD sed: -i ''
go build ./... && go vet ./...
\```

Guard: only string literals `"translate/…"` are import paths here; bare
`"translate"` occurrences were non-import string literals (a config subdir const
and a keybinding help string), so the prefixed replace was safe. Verify with
`grep -rn '"translate/' cmd internal main.go` returning nothing.

## Prevention

- When creating a Go project you might ever `go install`/publish, name the module
  after its future repo URL from day one: `go mod init github.com/<you>/<repo>`.
- Binary name from `go install` is the **last path element** of the main package's
  module path — keeping the repo named `translate` keeps the binary `translate`.

## Related

- Sibling: [gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir.md](gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir.md)
- Shipped fix: `TODO.md` Done "Publish as a public repo + `go install`"
- Go docs: https://go.dev/ref/mod#go-install

```

```md
# go install drops the binary in ~/.local/share/mise/installs/go/<ver>/bin (orphaned after mise Go upgrade)

**Symptoms** (grep this section): installed CLI "disappears" after a Go
upgrade; `go env GOBIN` shows `.../.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.26.4/bin`;
`go install` puts binaries next to the mise toolchain instead of `~/go/bin` or
`~/.local/bin`; a tool works today and is "not found" after `mise upgrade go`.
**First seen**: 2026-07
**Affects**: macOS/Linux with Go managed by **mise** and `GOBIN` inherited from
the mise env; any `go install`ed CLI meant to be persistent.
**Status**: workaround documented (pin `GOBIN` per install).

## Symptom

\```
$ go env GOPATH GOBIN
/Users/david/go
/Users/david/.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.26.4/bin
\```

`GOBIN` points into the **version-specific** mise install dir. A plain
`go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest` lands the binary at
`~/.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.26.4/bin/translate`. The next time mise bumps
Go (e.g. to `1.26.5`), that directory changes / is pruned and the binary is gone
from PATH — the "resident" tool silently vanishes.

## Root cause

The mise Go environment exports `GOBIN` set to the active toolchain's own
`bin` directory. `go install` honors `GOBIN` above `GOPATH/bin`, so installs
follow the toolchain version instead of landing in a stable, version-independent
location. `~/go/bin` (GOPATH/bin) *is* on PATH via the dotfiles
(`dot_config/shell/02_legacy_tools.sh`), but `GOBIN` overrides it.

## Workaround

Pin `GOBIN` to a stable, XDG-clean, already-on-PATH dir for the install:

\```sh
GOBIN="$HOME/.local/bin" go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest
\```

`~/.local/bin` is on PATH, survives Go upgrades, and keeps `~/go/bin` (which
already exists for the module cache) uncluttered. Bake this `GOBIN=` into any
chezmoi `run_onchange` / Justfile install recipe so it's deterministic rather
than dependent on the ambient mise env.

## Prevention

- Always set `GOBIN=$HOME/.local/bin` explicitly in install recipes for
  persistent tools; never rely on the ambient `GOBIN`.
- Sanity check after install: `command -v translate` should resolve to
  `~/.local/bin/translate`, not a `.../mise/installs/...` path.

## Related

- Sibling: [go-install-module-path-mismatch.md](go-install-module-path-mismatch.md)
- Sibling: [duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md](duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md)
- Shipped fix: `TODO.md` Done "Publish as a public repo + `go install`"

```

```md
# Two `translate` binaries: ~/.dotfiles/bin shadows ~/.local/bin (edits/reinstalls don't take effect)

**Symptoms** (grep this section): reinstalled or rebuilt `translate` but the
old behaviour persists; `command -v -a translate` prints two paths; a stale
`~/.dotfiles/bin/translate` wins over a fresh `~/.local/bin/translate`; version
mismatch between `which translate` and the just-installed binary.
**First seen**: 2026-07 (anticipated — no live collision yet)
**Affects**: this host's PATH order, where `~/.dotfiles/bin` precedes
`~/.local/bin`; triggered by the Justfile `install` recipe.
**Status**: workaround documented; real fix tracked in `TODO.md` P2.

## Symptom

The blessed install path is `GOBIN=~/.local/bin go install …`. But the
project `Justfile` also has:

\```make
install: build
    install -m 0755 translate ~/.dotfiles/bin/translate
\```

On this host PATH is ordered `… ~/.dotfiles/bin (pos 11) … ~/.local/bin (pos 13)`,
so if `just install` has ever run, the copy in `~/.dotfiles/bin` **shadows** the
`go install` copy:

\```
$ command -v -a translate
/Users/david/.dotfiles/bin/translate     # stale, wins
/Users/david/.local/bin/translate        # fresh, ignored
\```

You then rebuild / `go install` a new version, run `translate`, and see no
change — because a different, older binary earlier on PATH is what runs.

## Root cause

Two independent install mechanisms target two different PATH dirs, and
`~/.dotfiles/bin` (chezmoi-managed scripts dir) sorts before `~/.local/bin`.
Shell PATH resolution takes the first match, so whichever dir is earlier wins
regardless of which binary is newer.

## Workaround

Standardize on one location (`~/.local/bin` via `go install`) and remove the
stray copy:

\```sh
rm -f ~/.dotfiles/bin/translate
GOBIN="$HOME/.local/bin" go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest
command -v -a translate   # should print ONLY ~/.local/bin/translate
\```

## Prevention

- Fix the `Justfile` `install` recipe to `GOBIN=$HOME/.local/bin go install .`
  (or delete it) so it can't create a shadowing copy — tracked in `TODO.md` P2.
- After any install, verify with `command -v -a translate` that exactly one path
  resolves.

## Related

- Sibling: [gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir.md](gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir.md)
- Real fix: `TODO.md` P2 "Align `just install` with `go install` / `~/.local/bin`"

```
